programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The two Marine brothers of a sailor battling leukaemia are killed in the line of duty, and to spare the servicemen's family further grief, the secretary of the US Navy seeks the NCIS team's help in locating a suitable bone marrow donor. Meanwhile, Bishop returns home to Oklahoma for Thanksgiving. Military crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deeks places his job in danger when he shoots and kills an unarmed man during a mission to retrieve Semtex explosives from a marine warehouse, prompting unexpected emotions for Kensi as she contemplates losing her partner. Crime drama, starring Eric Christian Olsen and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are investigated by the FBI on suspicion of murdering arms dealer La Grenouille. programme.desc: Tracey decides to make an effort with her in-laws for Darryl's sake - but soon lives to regret it. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon tries to make the best of her job at the bakery and Dorien joins the Open University, while Tracey continues to study at the school of hard knocks. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Terry and Max when they find themselves stuck in a traffic jam and are helpless to intervene as carjackers drag a woman from her vehicle, stabbing her fatally. The atmosphere deteriorates as the detectives work on the case, which involves a large-scale international gang, and Sam has to separate them when they come to blows

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The day of the interviews for the consultancy role arrives, and Sahira's judgement is tested when a patient whose condition she had previously diagnosed as a benign tumour is readmitted with serious complications. Meanwhile, Malick is forced to suppress his aggressive personality while dealing with a man suffering from a stab wound, and Dan books a weekend away in Barcelona for himself and Chrissie - but his grand gesture misfires

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Selena returns to the hospital, and is annoyed to learn Maggie is no longer a consultant - and an associate specialist position has been advertised. Ellen tells Nina to move back home with Abs, and gives the pair a present to thank them for their support, while Kelsey tells her latest date she is an air stewardess

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sally and Beth discover a man found badly beaten had argued with his runaway daughter Lucy the night before. Beth develops a soft spot for the girl, but can't get much out of her about the incident and her touchingly protective boyfriend Mark (guest star Joe Absolom) denies suggestions he might be involved. The family clearly have a lot to hide and Sally warns Beth not to get personally involved

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: An adventure for Billy turns into a nightmare for Carol. Pauline is on the warpath with Tony. Peggy reaches the end of a long battle but her plans seem insensitive to Kathy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie secures a job interview at deals on wheels, Grant returns from Fiji and Billy is a prime witness in a court trial

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Tony is hauled over the coals for smuggling rifles into Britain, and Fitzpatrick blows his top on the eve of McCleod's funeral, angry that disillusioned widow Angela is not allowing it to be done with full military honours

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James, Siegfried and Tristan find different ways of dealing with their domestic problems, and Mrs Hall makes a surprise announcement. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer is asked to sell a sinister-looking mirror from a ballet school, and determines to finally marry Lady Jane. However, the object of his affections has some shocking news for him that suggests he may have left matters too late. Roger Lloyd Pack (Trigger in Only Fools and Horses) guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides the community needs an old folks' home, and takes her fight to the local council. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Harvey invites local students into Bayview to interact with the residents, and bribes one to deliberately annoy troublemaking resident Diana in the hope she will retaliate and be thrown out of the home

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley is almost ready to unveil his latest creation, but his odd behavour makes the trio want to find out what lies within his shed sooner than he would prefer. Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde, Bill Owen and Gordon Wharmby star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is stunned to catch Nora Batty in a church hall closet with Billy Chiselhurst. Realising he has a rival for her affections, he challenges Billy to a duel at dawn. Bill Owen, Kathy Staff and John Jardine star

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: When a body washes ashore, Jane is convinced it is a botched sea burial, but Davey believes otherwise. With a meal at stake, both are determined to prove each other wrong

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: A gunman begins murdering uniformed police officers, prompting the detective to taunt the killer on TV in the hope of inspiring him to turn his anger on him and come out into the open. To his horror, Luther also learns that Alice Morgan has been investigating his past - and is threatening to tell Zoe what she has discovered. Thriller, starring Idris Elba, Indira Varma, Steven Mackintosh and Saskia Reeves, with Ruth Wilson and Sean Pertwee. Written by Neil Cross (Spooks)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team is given the task of re-examining the unsolved case of peace protester Josh Livesey, found dead near a nuclear base in 1984. Other campaigners pleaded innocence at the time, but refused to co-operate with police, blaming the death on Secret Service skulduggery. Detective drama, starring Alun Armstrong, James Bolam, Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki travels to Mexico to attend the funeral of Luisa, a former intern at the Lyell Centre who was murdered in a carjacking in her native country. She soon stumbles across the post-mortem report and realises that the body is not that of her ex-colleague. Assisted by Dr Eva Vasquez and fired up with the idea that Luisa might still be alive, Nikki and Jack trace her last-known movements and are led to a farm where a cartel massacre was carried out. Crime drama, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A woman's corpse is discovered in a ploughed field, and the only lead is her husband, a potter. A year earlier, he suddenly decided to start a new life as a monk, suggesting that his wife may have committed suicide because she could not bear to lose him to the brethren - or that her spouse may have had a hand in her death. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

programme.desc: Nathan announces Selena's promotion to associate specialist, leaving Maggie unsure how to react. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Lucy Burton's body is found in an apparent suicide, but forensics reveal she was murdered. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tension increases at the Vic, while Rickie has some unwelcome visitors

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky gets arrested for his connection to stolen cars - but will he keep quiet regarding Phil's involvement?

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Drysdale's superiors blame him for McCleod's death, Fitzpatrick is turned down for promotion, and Andy's attempt to prove himself gets Jacko into trouble

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Herriot household becomes inceasingly hectic, Tristan contemplates putting his bachelor days behind him, and Siegfried's new assistant arrives - bringing his badger with him. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Left to look after Felsham Hall until it has been sold, Lovejoy uses the house as a furniture showroom. It seems unlikely anyone could replace Lady Jane in the dodgy dealer's affections - but that doesn't prevent his eye being caught by glamorous auctioneer Charlotte Cavendish. Charlie Gimbert eventually buys the property, and agrees to rent the stables for Lovejoy Antiques. Patricia Hayes guest stars in an episode directed by Ian McShane

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: The Antiques Roadshow comes to town, so William and Hester take along their Chinese vase to get a valuation. On their arrival back home the pair surprise a burglar, so Hester tries out her newly acquired martial arts skills. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's son Geoffrey summons the courage to walk out on his wife, and moves into Bayview instead - but after a few days of his boring company Diana hatches a plan to get rid of him

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler comes face to face with the woman of his dreams and promptly sweeps her off her feet - on to the back of his tandem, and Barry resolves to throw caution to the wind and show his affection for Glenda. Roy Clarke's long-running comedy, starring Jean Alexander, Thora Hird and Stephen Lewis alongside stalwarts Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy encounters an unlucky chimney sweep, and sets out to find him a good old-fashioned chimney to sweep intent on helping preserve the tradition. Comedy, starring Brain Wilde

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A horse trainer with money worries is found hanged, and suicide is suspected until tests reveal he was drugged shortly before his death. Lynley discovers the cove on the victim's estate was used to land contraband, and connects the death to a smuggling racket, but Havers suspects the answer lies with the local riding community. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dying man asks the team to clear the name of his son, a talented junior golfer who committed suicide after being implicated in the murder of a 10-year-old boy. Aiding the investigation is a former chief superintendent who admits she always had doubts about the teenager's guilt, but the committee members of the exclusive golf club where the victim's body was discovered prove reluctant to help the officers with their inquiries. Isla Blair and Cherie Lunghi guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki struggles to come to terms with life back home and seeks out fellow pathologist Sally Vaughan for support, only to learn she is nowhere to be found. National Crime Agency investigator Guy Bernhardt asks Nikki to help him by employing pathologist David Cannon, the chief suspect in Sally's disappearance. As Jack, Clarissa and Thomas share concerns about Nikki's wellbeing, a body found in a reservoir is linked to a murder case from 16 years previously. Return of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, with Julian Rhind-Tutt and Alex Macqueen

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Lucy Burton's body is found in an apparent suicide, but forensics reveal she was murdered. A distraught Beth has the unenviable task of quizzing her friends and searching the house, while Terry discovers the victim's sinister housemate Mark Lawrence (played by Joe Absolom) has a history of beguiling and controlling girls

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: The RMP begins a desperate search, as Tilbrook takes Markham to visit a very important prisoner and Ed receives intelligence that puts his life in danger. Alison confides in Honor about her affair as she finds herself unable to continue with her pregnancy

programme.desc: Tracey tries to help ex-con Desmond, but Sharon is convinced he is a lost cause - and she is soon proved right. Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke star

Former Coronation Street star Sean Wilson (Martin Platt) guests

programme.desc: New constables Leon Taylor, Millie Brown and Arun Ghir (Dominic Power, Clare Foster and Abhin Galeya) quickly get into their stride when a stolen cutting torch is used in a bank robbery. programme.desc: Tiffany returns from Spain, and Sanjay is back from India with his mother in tow. Mark has a chat with Martin about the disappearance of some money from Ruth's purse

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin is caught grafittiing on the Community Centre. Later, he and his friends burgle Carol's house. Tiffany moves back with Tony and Simon. Sanjay's mother moves in

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Finlay temporarily hangs up his stethoscope to intervene in a confrontation between Irish farm labourers and irate locals who blame them for a typhus outbreak, and resolves matters with a combination of tough talking and common sense. David Rintoul stars, with Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A wild dog causes havoc in the market at Skeldale, Tristan is charged with examining a consignment of sheep bound for Russia, and James acquires a new record player. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is threatened by a debt collector over a long outstanding sum and his hopes of meeting the repayment rest on a silver tankard, and hijacking a local auction administered by Charlotte Cavendish to obtain an antique piece of Japanese pottery. programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester arranges a short cross-Channel trip to France to help take William's mind off his financial worries - and the pair end up shopping in Boulogne for duty free goods. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A vicar suffers a nervous breakdown at a resident's funeral and Tom's son has trouble with his nymphomaniac wife. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Ivy receives an anonymous love letter and spends all her time trying to work out who sent it, little realising Compo, Clegg and Foggy already know. However, she soon rules out everyone except Smiler - and promptly resolves to put him straight once and for all

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard concocts a plan to allay Pearl's well-founded suspicions - he just needs to find someone as small as himself. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The judge presides over the case of a man who killed a paedophile, an act that Jo defends by arguing it protected further victims from abuse. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a bloodstained pair of hot pants results in the team being asked to reinvestigate the 1971 disappearance of a mother and her six-month-old son. Amanda Redman stars

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A woman is murdered and the forensic evidence takes the team to a cosmetic clinic where the victim had been receiving various treatments seemingly for free, but Nikki and Silva are frustrated by the CEO of Hamilton Ashe hospital when he blocks access to Karen's medical records. programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations team using their special off-road vehicle

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Leominster in Herefordshire, where she learns about the history of the local hat and glove industries. Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri are helping the teams

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's a big day for Valerie, but Karen has forgotten something important. A documentary crew visits the prison, and Daniel helps an inmate struggling to find a way out of the system

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards returns with the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps an artist to find a period property in Hampshire, and joins a tree-planting initiative to celebrate the 10th anniversary of the South Downs National Park

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, hoping to breathe new life into a leaking tent, a scandi style coffee table and a miniature greenhouse.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by a candidate for the London mayoral election.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a team of roving reporters present topical stories from around the UK

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rainie and Stuart weigh up the pros and cons of Bernie being a surrogate, Zack offers to help Nancy get a job and the Carters throw a surprise picnic for Shirley, where Linda overhears her daughter calling Tamwar. Billy meets Janet's agent Estelle, who takes an shine to him and invites him for a drink. Sue Holderness (Only Fools and Horses' Marlene) joins the cast

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A party of German guests comes to stay and Basil resolves to ensure everyone is on their best behaviour. Regrettably, a nasty case of concussion sustained during a fire drill ensures his plans come crashing down. Starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: First episode of a new sitcom, co-written by and starring Rose Matafeo. A drunken New Year's eve hook-up becomes far more complicated for Jessie when she discovers her one night stand is actually a film star. What she thought would become an amusing anecdote soon turns into something else

programme.title: Dreaming Whilst Black
programme.desc: One-off drama about a young aspiring filmmaker trapped in his recruitment job. When he bumps into an old friend from film school who is now working at a production company, he gets the chance that he has been waiting for. Unfortunately, she fixes him a once-in-a-lifetime pitching opportunity which clashes with a career-saving presentation - forcing him to choose between going all in or giving up his dream entirely. Co-written by and starring Adjani Salmon

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A return visit to a former pub in Measham, Leicestershire, that Martel Maxwell looked round in 2018, to find out if the owners have finally completed their ambitious project. Plus, properties in Portsmouth and the Cumbria town of Maryport

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood finds out what it takes to fill the UK's freezers with peas, joining the harvest on a Yorkshire farm and helping at the factory where they are bagged and frozen

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Ambulance crew Steve and John assess a patient in Glasgow who has suffered a suspected stroke, but she is reluctant to go to hospital. Colin and Dan deal with possible sepsis

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of grandmothers and granddaughters compete in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, under the guidance of John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Presenter Anita Manning gets 'into the groove' and has a go at record collecting

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's 50th birthday coincides with a local visit from a special celebrity guest - will she get to meet them? Emma and Luca's plans go awry and they end up sharing more than they expected

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple who want to make a new start in Devon, hoping to find a detached family home with a large garden for their two children and some income potential. Away from the search, Jules gets stuck into some woodcraft using nothing but steam to sculpt some impressive interior design pieces

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph scours Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey for quality items, uncovering a travel trunk, a 1940s utility cabinet and a dressing table with sentimental value

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restore a hand-carved model Spitfire, a leather pouffe and a silver purse containing treasured memories of the owner's mother

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Liberal Democrat candidate for mayor of London

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda makes a shocking discovery in Nancy's coat pocket, and when Frankie cannot get her to open up, she follows her to find out why she is being so mysterious. Kim is angry with Mila after seeing her with a man, convinced she is cheating on Iqra, and Bailey overhears Karen and Mitch talking about their financial concerns. As Janet's photo shoot comes to an end, Honey is surprised by a passing comment from another mother about how much the children get paid

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A conflicted Hanssen worries that the upcoming court case will cause more harm than good. Does his abuser still hold power over him? Ange's hopes of a quiet night shift are shattered when Dom and Sacha behave suspiciously and Fletch is daunted as he gets to grip with the hospital's disastrous finances

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate online dating scams, beginning with the story of a woman who was swept off her feet by a man she met over the internet. Things seemed to be going well but as soon as they started making plans to spend the rest of their lives together, the requests for money began. Also, DS Marc Cananur and his team uncover a treasure trove of designer goods and cash that might be the evidence needed to expose an elaborate fraud

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The gang have dognapped Frank's four-legged friend Duke, but soon realise it's not that easy sneaking an enormous Irish wolfhound past reception and up to their hotel room. Frank is distraught to hear his dog has gone missing until he receives a phone call from Keeley, promising to return the pooch if he gives them back their winnings. He will do anything for his dog - but Cheryl has other ideas. Meanwhile, Gemma is desperate to protect herself and her family from a sinister figure from the past

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants enter the world of film and TV as they create the looks for the costumed performers on US smash-hit drama Pose, with the winner having their make-up featured on the next series. In the creative brief, they must come up with a look that represents freedom, and the two weakest face off by creating the perfect red jewelled lip. Presented by Maya Jama

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: While contemplating how best to reconnect with her son, Norma receives a call from Nick Ford who claims he has Norman held in a metal box and that if she wants to see him alive, she has to get Dylan to kill Zane Morgan. Dylan considers the risk of carrying out the action. Vera Farmiga stars

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman finds he is unable to escape a tragic event from the past that continues to haunt him, and Norma works to ensure he does not make a terrible mistake. Meanwhile, Romero and Dylan try to bring the drug war to an end. Thriller prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's classic Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of properties in Carlisle in Cumbria, London's Forest Gate and Walsall in the West Midlands

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins the East Yorkshire bus company in Hull, getting behind the wheel of a double decker, making sure the vehicles go out on time and helping to keep them in perfect working order

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, paramedics Gary and Stephen help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a man with severe abdominal pain

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at Newark Antiques and Collectors Fair in Nottinghamshire, and presenter Charlie Ross learns about the Royal Observer Corps

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie kickstarts her investigation to track down the baby from the photo, while Rob is not too happy about the new sergeant at the police station. Jimmi tries to help a young lad with a stammer who has vital information for the police but is struggling to share it

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore explores Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, discovering a set of dining chairs with a matching table, a set of vintage lawn games, and some old aluminium lamp-shades

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse, while ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it. Plus, Will Kirk takes on the repair of a 3D wooden boat puzzle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By UKIP

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Kathy from Leicester presents the dealers with a catalogue for an auction to help people who lost loved ones in the Titanic disaster. Alice from Wales presents then with an unusual moulded men's underwear advert from the early 20th century - although the dealers are not so sure when they realise a bit is missing - and James from Stoke-on-Trent hopes to sell a pair of Victorian horse hair singers. Presented by Nigel Havers, with expert Simon Bower

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's gentlemen's classics week, and the sewers' skills are tested to the limit as they tackle headwear - a first for the show - in the shape of a Peaky Blinders-style baker boy cap, before being given 90 minutes to smarten up and transform second-hand men's jackets and blazers into a garment for a woman. In the made-to-measure challenge, they must create a casual utility jacket, with a relaxed fit and at least three pockets. Joe Lycett follows their progress

programme.title: Is Uni Racist?
programme.desc: Britain's universities are some of the most prestigious in the world, but are they safe places for students of colour? Linda Adey investigates the experiences of black and ethnic minority students and examines what happens when victims of racist abuse at university want to complain

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs, combining a mix of exciting new talent and returning contestants, compete for the North East, Yorkshire and Humber region with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. After their unique creations have been judged, one of them must leave the competition

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Plans are made to improve the mental health of the meerkats and wolverines, a koala has a nasty eye infection, and the flamingos show off their unusual nest building skills

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day 10 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of two second-round matches - Stuart Bingham v Jamie Jones, and David Gilbert v Judd Trump. At this stage last year, Martin Gould took on Kyren Wilson, and Barry Hawkins tested Neil Robertson's mettle. Fans also witnessed a gripping showdown between Anthony McGill and Jamie Clarke, as well as a clash between Ding Junhui and Ronnie O'Sullivan. It remains to be seen who will go through to the next stage in the 2021 tournament, but naturally all players are keeping their eyes on the prize

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo sets out on another railway adventure as he explores Britain between the world wars. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond, starting in the city of Oxford, where in 1921 women were awarded degrees for the first time. At Somerville College, Michael finds out about pioneering crystallographer Dorothy Hodgkin, the only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in chemistry

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day 10 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of two second-round matches - Shaun Murphy v Yan Bingtao, and Mark Allen v Mark Selby. Three-time champion Selby was pushed to the limit by Murphy's first-round conqueror Noppon Saengkham at this stage last year, eventually prevailing 13-12, but was eliminated in the semi-finals when he was on the wrong side of a deciding frame against Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions for the last time as six contestants take to the famous black chair in the final of the quiz, with specialist subjects of Shackleton's Antarctic expedition of 1914-17, Cole Porter, the Vienna Secession, Jimmy Carter, Flanders and Swann, and the films of David Cronenberg

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to the Anantara Al Jabal Al Akhdar in Oman, which at 2,000 metres above sea level is the highest 5-star hotel in the Middle East and is a resort seeking to blend local tradition with modern tastes. Led by the hotel's mountain guru Maher, Giles and Monica undertake a test-run of the adventure course before it opens to guests, facing 30-metre drops and Giles's profound fear of heights

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Only Connect presenter Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quizan extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Josh Widdicombe and author Emma Dabiri joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day 10 in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of the second round

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of second-round matches at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, which were played over the best of 25 frames

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher goes wild in the Hambleden Valley in Buckinghamshire, on a whistle-stop tour of a chocolate-box landscape that has been the backdrop for many a TV drama and film. He also finds out how the old cottage industry of lace-making is poised for a comeback, and saddles up to meet a farming couple opening up their farm to enable safer riding in this corner of the countryside. Adam Henson is busy with some new arrivals and, as picking season dawns, Charlotte Smith investigates whether UK farmers can cover a shortfall in the number of seasonal farm workers needed to gather their harvests

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Tupac Shakur's image is known around the world as he was one of hip-hop's most photographed figures. But could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we know? This edition throws a lens onto an extraordinary life by focusing on 10 defining pictures, from famous shots to private snaps, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the hip-hop star best

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Newby Hall, near Ripon in North Yorkshire, Treasures include a letter sent by Roald Dahl to a primary school, a Chippendale chair worth a king's ransom and football memorabilia harking back to the early days of the beautiful game - when a week's wages at Arsenal were less than the price of a cup of coffee today

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For their main courses, one chef is showcasing fallow deer in their dish celebrating the invention of cats eyes. Another pays homage to the Humber Bridge, and the final chef pays tribute to a miners' lamp with a dish centred on mutton. Whose inventiveness in the kitchen will veteran judge Simon Rogan rate the highest? Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the cottage garden at Longmeadow, Monty Don offers advice on what to plant under shrubs and roses. On a visit to Dunsborough Park in Surrey, Carol Klein celebrates a spectacular display of tulips, and Kate Bradbury goes through the first stages of creating a wildlife garden in Brighton

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Huddersfield Giants v St Helens, Wigan Warriors v Castleford Tigers and Hull Kingston Rovers v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session day 11 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the start of the quarter-finals. The opening two last-eight matches in 2020 were Mark Selby v Neil Robertson and Judd Trump v Kyren Wilson, with Selby and Wilson taking the honours

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session day 11 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the opening sessions of two quarter-finals, as eight frames take place in each. Ronnie O'Sullivan won his opening last-eight session 6-2 against Mark Williams last year and went on to win the title

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin go toe to toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through England's Home Counties at Stoke Mandeville in Buckinghamshire, where he discovers the legacy of a German Jewish doctor who fled the Nazis to settle in Britain. He then heads through the Chiltern Hills to Princes Risborough, before reaching Beaconsfield and revisiting his much-loved childhood haunt, Bekonscot Model Village

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening's play on day 11 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two quarter-finals continue with the second and penultimate sessions. Eight frames are due to be played in both contests, with the players returning tomorrow to play up to nine more, as the quest to win the title claimed by Ronnie O'Sullivan in 2020 gathers pace

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: The six remaining contestants return to the workshop to face two more challenges, making a cocktail ring before designing a bold bib necklace for drag queen Amrou to wear to a solo show. Who will impress the judges enough to stay, and who will have to leave?

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: Makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores what the beauty looks of three iconic moments in British history reveal about that era, continuing with the Victorians. In Victorian Britain, makeup was frowned upon, and women were supposed to be naturally beautiful, reflecting their inner purity. Throughout the programme, Lisa delves into this obsession with natural beauty and learns about some of the products created to help achieve this, before creating her own 'no makeup' look inspired by Victorian beauty

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Desiree Burch hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Paul McCaffrey and Jonny Pelham

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day 11 in Sheffield, featuring action from two quarter-finals

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the quarter-finals on day 11 at the Crucible Theatre

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: Documentary examining corruption in the police in the 1970s, revealing a secret network of officers was operating illegally throughout London and led to the formation of the first internal anti-corruption unit A10, which inspired the BBC drama Line of Duty. The story begins in 1969, when a desperate south London criminal tipped off the Times about how he was being extorted for money by a detective in the Metropolitan Police

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Documentary following the climate activist over the course of 2019 and 2020, as she travels the world exploring the science of global warming and challenging world leaders to take action on the growing crisis. The programme follows the impact of Covid-19 on her campaign, and the challenges of persuading a world living through one crisis to face up to the possibility of another on the horizon. In the first programme, Greta travels through North America on her way to a UN climate conference in Chile

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county,. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom finds out what it takes to fill the UK's freezers with peas, joining in with the harvest on a Yorkshire farm and helping at the factory where they are bagged and frozen

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph scours Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey for quality items, uncovering a travel trunk, a 1940s utility cabinet and a dressing table with sentimental value

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the North East, Yorkshire and Humber region go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. Guest judge Sophie Conran, cook, author and designer, helps decide the winner. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A Scottish dog lover showcases a range of natural doggie treats, hoping Dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies and Touker Suleyman will throw her a financial bone. Others braving the heat of the Den include a beauty entrepreneur with her range of affordable-yet-luxury waterless face oils and a Northumbrian inventor hoping to demonstrate his nifty construction bracket. Plus, a tech entrepreneur is put through her paces when she showcases fitness app aimed solely at women. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where one quarter-final continues and another concludes

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where one quarter-final continues and another concludes

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in North West London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two quarter-finals conclude and the line-up for the last four is confirmed. The top three seeds all fell in the last eight of 2020, with Judd Trump, Neil Robertson and Mark Williams losing to Kyren Wilson, Mark Selby and Ronnie O'Sullivan respectively

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Paul Cunningham, Phelim O'Hagan, Gemma Austin and Andy Scollick compete for Northern Ireland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Given Belfast's renowned connection with the Titanic, there is much luxury maritime inspiration to be drawn, but the chefs also plunder Northern Ireland's other long and varied list of inventions

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: The final instalment reveals how, in the late 1970s, corruption within the City of London's CID had grown out of control - and how Operation Countryman was launched in response

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Matt Lucas, Jerry Springer and Cariad Lloyd join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'noodles' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day 12 in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of the quarter-finals

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Quarter-final action on day 12 from Sheffield's Crucible Theatre, where the world's elite have gathered

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: Police are called after a wealthy man suspected to be linked to drug crime was taken from his home in central Manchester by an armed gang. After locating the address where the victim was held and tortured, forensic evidence leads officers to name suspects, but while the hunt is on to find these dangerous criminals, another horrific kidnap happens

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan scours the globe for solutions to environmental problems, examining the natural and technological fixes that can help people slow the rate of decline and adapt to shifts already taking place. He begins in the Solomon Islands and then travels down the east coast of Australia from the Great Barrier Reef to Tasmania, learning about the 'feminisation' of green turtles and coming face-to-face with the terror of a bushfire

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray create a bargain border from scratch, Kirsty Wilson creates a recycled birdhouse with its own living roof, and Brian Cunningham and his family make family-friendly, no-dig vegetable plots in the lawn

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, and uses their creations to make a tortilla dish with caramelised apples and onions. In the fishing village of Mevagissey, he discovers the origins of the sea shanty and the history of the pilchard fishing industry, when shoals of pilchards were so large they would attract killer whales

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: As the Channel Tunnel's 25th anniversary collides with Brexit, meet the teams behind the scenes and beneath the seabed who keep everything on track through the longest undersea tunnel in the world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumn landscape in a palette of exotic orange and gold colours

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session on day 10 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of two second-round matches - Mark Allen v Mark Selby, and Shaun Murphy v Yan Bingtao. Three-time champion Selby was pushed to the limit by Shaun Murphy's first-round conqueror Noppon Saengkham at this stage last year, eventually prevailing 13-12, but was eliminated in the semi-finals when he was on the wrong side of a deciding frame against Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: Lights Up
programme.desc: Yasmin Joseph's play set during the 2017 Notting Hill Carnival, following two women fighting to preserve tradition and their own personal space. Filmed at the Harold Pinter Theatre

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people

programme.title: Colour: The Spectrum of Science
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski tells the story of scientific endeavour, revealing the secrets of the universe as she takes to the skies in a jumbo jet, equipped with an infra-red telescope. She explores how the birds and the bees, who can see ultraviolet, view the world, and looks at how x-rays can be used to harness colours and image the human body as never before, thereby opening up new ways to treat disease

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumn landscape in a palette of exotic orange and gold colours

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: As the Channel Tunnel's 25th anniversary collides with Brexit, meet the teams behind the scenes and beneath the seabed who keep everything on track through the longest undersea tunnel in the world

programme.title: Screengrabbed: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez presents a selection of short videos offering artists and film-makers the chance to tackle life's biggest topics. How do you know you're in love? What does it mean to be British? Illustrator and performer Jessie Cave and visual artist Sarah Maple are just two of those who give their refreshing take on these and other questions

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: With up to 400 trains a day running on its 62 miles of track, the Channel Tunnel is the busiest railway system in the world. And doing things at the double is key to its success. Two countries built it. There are two terminals. And there is duplication at both ends. This episode reveals how teams of two work on both sides of the Channel to keep tourists and truckers moving. But how will preparations for Brexit affect such a well-oiled system of twinning and duplication, finely tuned over 25 years?

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross gives more painting tips as he captures a snow-covered winter wonderland on canvas

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening's play on day 11 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two quarter-finals continue with the second and penultimate sessions. Eight frames are due to be played in both contests, with the players returning tomorrow to play up to nine more, as the quest to win the title claimed by Ronnie O'Sullivan in 2020 gathers pace

programme.title: Inside Chernobyl's Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary following the construction of a 36,000 tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 disaster. A team of international engineers race to build the new steel structure, but radiation levels make it almost impossible for workers to build right on top of the old reactor. The team face difficult challenges as they attempt to erect this new steel mega-structure and move it into place

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The construction of HMS Astute, the Royal Navy's biggest and most powerful attack submarine, which cost more than £1billion and is able to circumnavigate the globe without surfacing. The programme follows engineers, designers and skilled craftsmen as they work on the vessel, from the initial stages of production to its first journey into the open sea

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How variations on the theme of family are depicted in Australian movie-making The Castle's nuclear, if unorthodox family, a family of faith in The Devil's Playground, Romper Stomper's frightening Neo-Nazis and crime families such as those depicted in Ned Kelly and Animal Kingdom

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross gives more painting tips as he captures a snow-covered winter wonderland on canvas

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the structure in its 25th anniversary year, following the teams who keep everything on track through the undersea tunnel. Workers in the tunnel paint two giant murals that will link to each other across the sea, and the catenary team head out in their van to look for repairs. Inside the tunnel, the high-speed trains are being slowed down by the air they push in front of them, and the only way to relieve the pressure is with tubes connecting one tunnel to another

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross leads viewers into the beauty of the dry lands as he creates an oval painting for beginners and accomplished artists as well

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two quarter-finals conclude and the line-up for the last four is confirmed. programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: A focus on the so-called Saturday Night Massacre, the chain of events that unfolded on October 20, 1973 when President Nixon demanded that special prosecutor Archibald Cox be fired

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: The documentary examines how mounting evidence against Richard Nixon led to his resignation. After compelling evidence was found on the automatic taping system in the Oval Office, the House voted for impeachment and the president resigned

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in real time and without voiceover or music, this series examines the crafting of handmade objects. The first programme observes expert Michael Ruh carrying out the time-consuming process of blowing and rolling glass to make a simple jug. Glass is heated in a steel crucible until it becomes liquid. Ruh's task is to keep a globule hot and in constant motion as he shapes it

Inside Giovanna’s bag is are some scented lavender bags, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are smelly. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is sending a letter in the post to the CBeebies house. She finds out how posting a letter works as she follows the letter's journey from the post box to the sorting office and finally to the house. Using a special infrared light, Maddie shows us how stamps are scanned to tell if they are first or second class. Next, she visits a factory where colouring pencils are made. She finds out how the coloured crayon inside is made from clay and, using her slow-motion camera, we see the wooden shaft of the pencil being made.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Oakley, Arthur, Luna, Reau, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a toy camera, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around a day out. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate toys and objects inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song Looking Out on Our Day Out features, as does a story involving Baby Bear. Viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring things that are themed around a day out too. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: It’s the annual dance festival in the garden, and Santiago the Smooth Newt is getting his groove on. But the problem is, his groove isn’t really up to scratch. He wants to take part, but he just hasn’t got the moves. He spots Walter and Winnie, the water vole twins, but they’re too busy running about to help him. Jim the Duck and his friend aren’t much help either as they’re practicing their synchronised swimming. Santiago is losing hope, until wise old Auntie the Tawny Owl gives him some great advice. Perhaps Santiago isn’t such a bad mover after all.

Alexander starts at home, playing a game using balls and two hula hoops - what does he get up to? Cianna plays in the garden with her family, having a go at stacking colourful rings - but does she know what order they go in? Alexander heads out to play his very first game of mini golf. His club is as tall as he is! Meanwhile, Cianna and her brother have some splashing fun at a local park before collecting some leaves to do leaf printing at home. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is having a party! She finds out how bubbles work when we blow into bubble mixture. She shows us how the surface tension of water changes when we add soap and how we can stop bubbles from bursting with our hands, and using her slow-motion camera, she shows how bubbles make a sphere shape. Next, Maddie heads to a factory where a party favourite is made - chocolate! We learn how chocolate starts out as a fruit, and we see the chocolates being made for an assortment box. Maddie also uses her special camera to see the super-fast robots that put the different chocolates into the right holes in their boxes.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A half-asleep Love Monster exits his house and walks towards the green, passing snoozing citizens on the way. He notices that the whole of the green is covered with litter and snoring Fluffytowners. Love Monster remembers that yesterday was Woo Hoo Day and the whole of Fluffytown had a big party - which makes today the Day After Woo Hoo Day. Love Monster attempts to clean up all of the mess on his own as a nice surprise for his friends, but he struggles and soon realises that it is a very big job. He should ask for help! He wakes everyone up, saying if everyone picks up one piece of litter each, they can tidy up the green in no time. Elder Kitten says he knows just the song for the job. Love Monster and Elder Kitten lead the Fluffytowners in singing a funky, tidying up song as they each pick up a piece of litter to clean the green. Everyone takes their piece of litter to the recycling centre where they queue up to do their bit for the environment.

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast

programme.desc: One-off drama about a young aspiring filmmaker trapped in his recruitment job. programme.desc: While contemplating how best to reconnect with her son, Norma receives a call from Nick Ford who claims he has Norman held in a metal box and that if she wants to see him alive, she has to get Dylan to kill Zane Morgan. Dylan considers the risk of carrying out the action. Vera Farmiga stars

programme.desc: Norman finds he is unable to escape a tragic event from the past that continues to haunt him, and Norma works to ensure he does not make a terrible mistake.
