programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By UKIP

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's gentlemen's classics week, and the sewers' skills are tested to the limit as they tackle headwear - a first for the show - in the shape of a Peaky Blinders-style baker boy cap, before being given 90 minutes to smarten up and transform second-hand men's jackets and blazers into a garment for a woman. In the made-to-measure challenge, they must create a casual utility jacket, with a relaxed fit and at least three pockets. Joe Lycett follows their progress

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Is Uni Racist?
programme.desc: Britain's universities are some of the most prestigious in the world, but are they safe places for students of colour? Linda Adey investigates the experiences of black and ethnic minority students and examines what happens when victims of racist abuse at university want to complain

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations team using their special off-road vehicle

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Leominster in Herefordshire, where she learns about the history of the local hat and glove industries. Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri are helping the teams

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's a big day for Valerie, but Karen has forgotten something important. A documentary crew visits the prison, and Daniel helps an inmate struggling to find a way out of the system

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards returns with the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps an artist to find a period property in Hampshire, and joins a tree-planting initiative to celebrate the 10th anniversary of the South Downs National Park

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, hoping to breathe new life into a leaking tent, a scandi style coffee table and a miniature greenhouse.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By Reform UK

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a team of roving reporters present topical stories from around the UK

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers check head to the East of Scotland to check out properties in Aberdeenshire and St Andrews, including a rural farm steading conversion, a silvery city apartment, and a home with a garden at its heart

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rainie and Stuart weigh up the pros and cons of Bernie being a surrogate, Zack offers to help Nancy get a job and the Carters throw a surprise picnic for Shirley, where Linda overhears her daughter calling Tamwar. Billy meets Janet's agent Estelle, who takes an shine to him and invites him for a drink. Sue Holderness (Only Fools and Horses' Marlene) joins the cast

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A party of German guests comes to stay and Basil resolves to ensure everyone is on their best behaviour. Regrettably, a nasty case of concussion sustained during a fire drill ensures his plans come crashing down. Starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler explain and analyse the latest news from the General Election campaign trail

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: First episode of a new sitcom, co-written by and starring Rose Matafeo. A drunken New Year's eve hook-up becomes far more complicated for Jessie when she discovers her one night stand is actually a film star. What she thought would become an amusing anecdote soon turns into something else

programme.title: Dreaming Whilst Black
programme.desc: One-off drama about a young aspiring filmmaker trapped in his recruitment job. When he bumps into an old friend from film school who is now working at a production company, he gets the chance that he has been waiting for. Unfortunately, she fixes him a once-in-a-lifetime pitching opportunity which clashes with a career-saving presentation - forcing him to choose between going all in or giving up his dream entirely. Co-written by and starring Adjani Salmon

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A return visit to a former pub in Measham, Leicestershire, that Martel Maxwell looked round in 2018, to find out if the owners have finally completed their ambitious project. Plus, properties in Portsmouth and the Cumbria town of Maryport

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood finds out what it takes to fill the UK's freezers with peas, joining the harvest on a Yorkshire farm and helping at the factory where they are bagged and frozen

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Ambulance crew Steve and John assess a patient in Glasgow who has suffered a suspected stroke, but she is reluctant to go to hospital. Colin and Dan deal with possible sepsis

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of grandmothers and granddaughters compete in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, under the guidance of John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Presenter Anita Manning gets 'into the groove' and has a go at record collecting

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's 50th birthday coincides with a local visit from a special celebrity guest - will she get to meet them? Emma and Luca's plans go awry and they end up sharing more than they expected

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple who want to make a new start in Devon, hoping to find a detached family home with a large garden for their two children and some income potential. Away from the search, Jules gets stuck into some woodcraft using nothing but steam to sculpt some impressive interior design pieces

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph scours Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey for quality items, uncovering a travel trunk, a 1940s utility cabinet and a dressing table with sentimental value

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restore a hand-carved model Spitfire, a leather pouffe and a silver purse containing treasured memories of the owner's mother

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda makes a shocking discovery in Nancy's coat pocket, and when Frankie cannot get her to open up, she follows her to find out why she is being so mysterious. Kim is angry with Mila after seeing her with a man, convinced she is cheating on Iqra, and Bailey overhears Karen and Mitch talking about their financial concerns. As Janet's photo shoot comes to an end, Honey is surprised by a passing comment from another mother about how much the children get paid

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A conflicted Hanssen worries that the upcoming court case will cause more harm than good. Does his abuser still hold power over him? Ange's hopes of a quiet night shift are shattered when Dom and Sacha behave suspiciously and Fletch is daunted as he gets to grip with the hospital's disastrous finances

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate online dating scams, beginning with the story of a woman who was swept off her feet by a man she met over the internet. Things seemed to be going well but as soon as they started making plans to spend the rest of their lives together, the requests for money began. Also, DS Marc Cananur and his team uncover a treasure trove of designer goods and cash that might be the evidence needed to expose an elaborate fraud

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The gang have dognapped Frank's four-legged friend Duke, but soon realise it's not that easy sneaking an enormous Irish wolfhound past reception and up to their hotel room. Frank is distraught to hear his dog has gone missing until he receives a phone call from Keeley, promising to return the pooch if he gives them back their winnings. He will do anything for his dog - but Cheryl has other ideas. Meanwhile, Gemma is desperate to protect herself and her family from a sinister figure from the past

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler present the latest news and analysis from the election campaign

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants enter the world of film and TV as they create the looks for the costumed performers on US smash-hit drama Pose, with the winner having their make-up featured on the next series. In the creative brief, they must come up with a look that represents freedom, and the two weakest face off by creating the perfect red jewelled lip. Presented by Maya Jama

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: While contemplating how best to reconnect with her son, Norma receives a call from Nick Ford who claims he has Norman held in a metal box and that if she wants to see him alive, she has to get Dylan to kill Zane Morgan. programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Dylan takes Ruby out on a date and works up the courage to tell her how he feels, while Rebecca's lies finally catchup with her, an anxious Lou prepares for her interview, and Alex has a difficult decision to make

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Kathy from Leicester presents the dealers with a catalogue for an auction to help people who lost loved ones in the Titanic disaster. Alice from Wales presents then with an unusual moulded men's underwear advert from the early 20th century - although the dealers are not so sure when they realise a bit is missing - and James from Stoke-on-Trent hopes to sell a pair of Victorian horse hair singers. Presented by Nigel Havers, with expert Simon Bower

programme.desc: The team restore a hand-carved model Spitfire, a leather pouffe and a silver purse containing treasured memories of the owner's mother

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda makes a shocking discovery in Nancy's coat pocket, and when Frankie cannot get her to open up, she follows her to find out why she is being so mysterious. Kim is angry with Mila after seeing her with a man, convinced she is cheating on Iqra, and Bailey overhears Karen and Mitch talking about their financial concerns. As Janet's photo shoot comes to an end, Honey is surprised by a passing comment from another mother about how much the children get paid

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A conflicted Hanssen worries that the upcoming court case will cause more harm than good. Does his abuser still hold power over him? Ange's hopes of a quiet night shift are shattered when Dom and Sacha behave suspiciously and Fletch is daunted as he gets to grip with the hospital's disastrous finances

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate online dating scams, beginning with the story of a woman who was swept off her feet by a man she met over the internet. Things seemed to be going well but as soon as they started making plans to spend the rest of their lives together, the requests for money began. Also, DS Marc Cananur and his team uncover a treasure trove of designer goods and cash that might be the evidence needed to expose an elaborate fraud

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The gang have dognapped Frank's four-legged friend Duke, but soon realise it's not that easy sneaking an enormous Irish wolfhound past reception and up to their hotel room. Frank is distraught to hear his dog has gone missing until he receives a phone call from Keeley, promising to return the pooch if he gives them back their winnings. He will do anything for his dog - but Cheryl has other ideas. Meanwhile, Gemma is desperate to protect herself and her family from a sinister figure from the past

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary charts how John DeLorean's dream of building the car of the future in Belfast went horribly wrong in an almost unbelievable tale of corporate greed and a $30million FBI cocaine sting - leading to the car-maker's downfall

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants enter the world of film and TV as they create the looks for the costumed performers on US smash-hit drama Pose, with the winner having their make-up featured on the next series. In the creative brief, they must come up with a look that represents freedom, and the two weakest face off by creating the perfect red jewelled lip. Presented by Maya Jama

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: While contemplating how best to reconnect with her son, Norma receives a call from Nick Ford who claims he has Norman held in a metal box and that if she wants to see him alive, she has to get Dylan to kill Zane Morgan. Dylan considers the risk of carrying out the action. Vera Farmiga stars

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of properties in Carlisle in Cumbria, London's Forest Gate and Walsall in the West Midlands

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins the East Yorkshire bus company in Hull, getting behind the wheel of a double decker, making sure the vehicles go out on time and helping to keep them in perfect working order

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, paramedics Gary and Stephen help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a man with severe abdominal pain

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at Newark Antiques and Collectors Fair in Nottinghamshire, and presenter Charlie Ross learns about the Royal Observer Corps

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie kickstarts her investigation to track down the baby from the photo, while Rob is not too happy about the new sergeant at the police station. Jimmi tries to help a young lad with a stammer who has vital information for the police but is struggling to share it

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore explores Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, discovering a set of dining chairs with a matching table, a set of vintage lawn games, and some old aluminium lamp-shades

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse, while ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it. Plus, Will Kirk takes on the repair of a 3D wooden boat puzzle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Jo Scott lends a hand on a daffodil farm in Co Tyrone as preparations are made for spring, and Gavin Andrews travels to Portballintrae to meet the locals keeping the coastal village's maritime heritage alive. Ruth Sanderson finds out more about the mysterious reappearance of native European oysters on the shores after more than 100 years and Gavin takes a trip to an old war relic on Lough Neagh that has become home to wildlife

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's gentlemen's classics week, and the sewers' skills are tested to the limit as they tackle headwear - a first for the show - in the shape of a Peaky Blinders-style baker boy cap, before being given 90 minutes to smarten up and transform second-hand men's jackets and blazers into a garment for a woman. In the made-to-measure challenge, they must create a casual utility jacket, with a relaxed fit and at least three pockets. Joe Lycett follows their progress

programme.title: Is Uni Racist?
programme.desc: Britain's universities are some of the most prestigious in the world, but are they safe places for students of colour? Linda Adey investigates the experiences of black and ethnic minority students and examines what happens when victims of racist abuse at university want to complain

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman finds he is unable to escape a tragic event from the past that continues to haunt him, and Norma works to ensure he does not make a terrible mistake. Meanwhile, Romero and Dylan try to bring the drug war to an end. Thriller prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's classic Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Commmittee's session on moving animals across borders, from Tuesday 20 April.

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: We explore how biases built into everyday technologies impact people's lives and how the tech industry is trying to address it. We go behind the scenes looking at how facial recognition algorithms work. And in a world where machines help recruiters find candidates for jobs, how do we make sure certain communities don't end up disadvantaged?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Tuesday in Parliament
programme.desc: Presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

Nada Tawfik reports on the outcome and the impact of one of the most important trials America has ever witnessed.

programme.desc: When a butterfly comes in the window of the crèche and lands on Sula’s painting, Bing decides that he will help the poor thing find its way back outside

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: Although the Blobfish looks rather unusual, he says that there are other fish who are even more aesthetically displeasing than him

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: A new spell causes problems for Tom, but Muru knows how to give him the confidence he needs

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: A big day awaits as a new friend arrives in the garden

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Morag Ann MacNeil reads A' Choinean Chòir by Katie Cotton, illustrated by Blanca Gomez and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: Po is excited to meet a famous wrestler - but finds he is not quite as heroic as he appears

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: The competition is heating up today - Coinneach Clogaid steals a bicycle and Olaidh Orains meets new friends

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Kerry Anne Macleod and Allan MacDonald conduct more fun science experiments, including watching objects disappear without a trace, and making salt to sprinkle on fish and chips

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Stornoway-based vet Joanna Jackson is busy with lambing in Ness and Barvas, while in Aberdeenshire Scott Rigg is in surgery with a Bernese mountain dog and a collie

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Ross Christie talks about how he came across the work of little-known poet Katharine Whyte Grant from Argyll. He reads her poem Blàith-Fhleasg o Mhòd nam Maca-Meamna

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: An honest depiction of the highs and lows of lockdown from all walks of life, including a musician who is missing performing on stage and a first-year student at university

programme.title: Sar-sgeoil: Outlander
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald explores the locations, folklore and history that inspired Diana Gabaldon's hugely successful Outlander, outlining the facts behind the fiction that has brought fans flocking to Scotland from across the globe

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon follows in Rob Roy's footsteps in Stirlingshire's hills and glens. Starting in Callander, the walk takes Ramsay to Samson's Stone

programme.title: Of Diabolical Purpose - Sgeulachd Monsieur Chantrelle
programme.desc: Using dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion, John Morrison revisits the story of Eugene Chantrelle, a French teacher hanged for murdering his wife in Edinburgh in 1878. A gifted linguist with medical knowledge, he frequently threatened to poison his wife - and was accused of so doing upon her death. Chantrelle pleaded his innocence up to the moment he was hanged and the evidence against him was circumstancial - and his great-grandson speaks of the lasting damage caused to the family

programme.title: Na Trads
programme.desc: Another chance to hear music by The Scott Wood Band, Session A9, Tryst, Chris Stout & Catriona MacKay and Kinnaris Quintet, amongst others

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Sally Lindsay hosts as five family and friends from Cheshire attempt to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Perry, Ros, Anna and Ben pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Millie is rushed to hospital on Kim's watch, Faith emboldens Nate, and Nicola conspires behind Jimmy's back

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sharon sets her plan into motion, Dev arranges a summit meeting with Corey's father, and Chesney acts as peacemaker for Fiz and Tyrone

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan is joined by Twiggy, who pays tribute to the Great British seaside, while horticulturist David Domoney selects garden furniture to help people entertain this summer. Plus, Alan goes behind the scenes at one of the UK's leading farm parks

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sharon steals from Gail as pressure mounts. Dev drives Asha further towards Corey. George disapproves of Todd's business methods

programme.title: Viewpoint
programme.desc: Manchester primary school teacher Gemma Hillman goes missing and DCs Martin Young and Stella Beckett are given the task of putting her boyfriend - the prime suspect - under surveillance. Clarke sets up his observation post in the home of single mum and secret voyeur Zoe Sterling, whose windows offer a panoramic view of Westbury Square and the home Gemma shared with Greg Sullivan. Crime drama, starring Noel Clarke, Alexandra Roach and Bronagh Waugh

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Accused of Murdering Our Son - The Steven Clark Story
programme.desc: Steven Clark, 23, disappeared in mysterious circumstances following a family walk with his mother on the 28th December, 1992. In September 2020, his parents Doris, 81, and Charles, 78, were arrested on suspicion of murder following a review by police from the Cleveland and North Yorkshire Cold Case Unit. Presenter and ex-detective Mark Williams-Thomas takes a look at the case

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andrea bans Kim from seeing Millie. Rhona has an idea. Dawn and Jamie meet for a drink - he wants to try again

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott embarks on a journey across the Mediterranean on a 44ft catamaran, preparing a variety of delicious recipes along the way. In this first edition, skipper Jean-Hughes teaches him the basics of sailing, and they drop anchor in a crystal-clear cove on the island of Lavezzi. Next, the chef travels to medieval fortress town of Bonifacio where he meets JB, a rugby player-turned-olive oil maker, as he samples his much sought-after oils before whipping up some cheesy stuffed aubergines

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Viewpoint
programme.desc: Martin witnesses Greg coming to pieces in front of his eyes. But is this a guilty man trying to hide his secrets or a grieving partner on a mission to find Gemma? Crime drama, starring Noel Clarke, Bronagh Waugh and Alexandra Roach

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: On Assignment
programme.desc: Rageh Omaar is in Mali for the hugely popular Hippodrome horseraces, exploring the country's deep historical links to horses and how the races are an important lifeline to many. Meanwhile, Lucy Watson is in Bosnia, where she meets a survivor of the Srebrenica genocide who has made it his life's mission to find the remains of those that did not survive

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. It's the start of the new year, and after their busy festive period, the hotel has high hopes for 2020 - looking at how they can improve all areas of their service and make it the most successful year in the hotel's history. Head butler Sean is determined to make his newest recruit - 37-year-old former actor Michael - the perfect Savoy butler, and is putting him to the test. As well as having to juggle a multitude of requests, Sean wants Michael to host an exclusive cocktail party for regular VIP guests, Mr and Mrs Cuming, in their suite

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: All hands are on deck as the metro system prepares for the busiest day of the year - the Great North Run. This year, a new and untested station in South Shields is at the centre of things. One of the event's regular runners prepares for a personal return to the course, as he runs in tribute to a friend

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Alan, Laura, Kerry and Enyioma pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By UKIP

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim gives Gabby a life lesson. Jimmy is delighted to be invited to the police station. Faith comes up with a plan

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sharon helps Sarah to throw Sam a birthday party. Asha enjoys living with Corey. Carla and Peter take advantage of an empty house

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets a pair of shih tzus from a puppy farm that need to learn all about domestic life, and an American bulldog struggling to cope without its owner. Later, he is introduced to a stray that has been living at Battersea for two years

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sam takes Sharon into his confidence. Peter receives great news about his transplant. Kevin and Abi are shocked at Tyrone's presumption

programme.title: Viewpoint
programme.desc: Martin goes on a mission to dig deeper into Carl Tuckman's murky past, hoping to find clues that may relate to Gemma's disappearance. Crime drama, starring Noel Clarke, Bronagh Waugh and Alexandra Roach

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.desc: Storms ruin the town's annual lobster festival, giving Bob an opportunity to become a local hero by letting disappointed attendees shelter in his restaurant. programme.desc: Peter reads an erotic novel and decides he can write a better one. However, Carter is sued for releasing the book on tape, and loses all his money, prompting his wife to run away with media mogul Ted Turner. Meanwhile, Stewie decides to train for the Olympics

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian gets a job with prestigious magazine The New Yorker, but it is not long before his new employer discovers he did not graduate from college, and promptly fires him. Filled with a burning desire to vindicate himself, he resolves to finish his education

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois thinks Stewie is trying to kill her because he is not spending enough time with his father. She tells Peter to make an effort to bond with the youngster - but a theme park outing ends in disaster

programme.desc: Animated sitcom about a floundering family-run burger restaurant. Louise drags Tina and Gene off on a secret mission to search an abandoned sweet factory for hidden treasure. Featuring the voices of H Jon Benjamin, Kristen Schaal and Eugene Mirman, with a guest performance by Cyndi Lauper

programme.desc: Brian's cousin Jasper announces he is to tie the knot with his boyfriend and the Griffins' house is to be the venue. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois discovers that she has an estranged brother, and combs the country in search of him, eventually finding him in a psychiatric hospital. Meanwhile, Peter starts a society for fat men. Animation, featuring the guest voice of Robert Downey Jr

programme.desc: A brush with death gives Brian a new lease of life, which he capitalises on by becoming a singer and performing with Frank Sinatra Jr. Meanwhile, Meg finally finds acceptance by pretending to be a lesbian

programme.desc: Brian gets a job with prestigious magazine The New Yorker, but it is not long before his new employer discovers he did not graduate from college, and promptly fires him. Filled with a burning desire to vindicate himself, he resolves to finish his education

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois thinks Stewie is trying to kill her because he is not spending enough time with his father. She tells Peter to make an effort to bond with the youngster - but a theme park outing ends in disaster

programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna insists the family helps at the homeless shelter at Christmas, but Cleveland is surprised to find the food there is better than what he eats at home. programme.desc: Storms ruin the town's annual lobster festival, giving Bob an opportunity to become a local hero by letting disappointed attendees shelter in his restaurant. programme.desc: Lois discovers that she has an estranged brother, and combs the country in search of him, eventually finding him in a psychiatric hospital. Meanwhile, Peter starts a society for fat men. Animation, featuring the guest voice of Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre spends her first night in prison, and Emily produces posters, alerting people to her plight. Ken visits Deirdre and tells her they are fighting for an appeal

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham tries to cover his tracks, a search party sets out to find Rachel, and Kathy comforts Graham when Rachel's death is announced

programme.desc: Paddy keeps Graham company at Mill Cottage. Roy asks Biff to be his best man. Eric is suspicious of Graham. Jack and Chris discuss the funeral arrangements

programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: There is trouble in the village when local children get their hands on some wartime ammunition, while Greengrass locks horns with Lord Ashfordly's new gamekeeper and Kate realises she is pregnant

programme.desc: Viv and Kelly badger Roy about the honeymoon. Kathy gives a eulogy at Rachel's funeral. Eric is convinced that Graham is up to no good. Alan makes a decision about selling the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric explains his suspicions about Graham to Stella. Mandy urges Roy to sort out the honeymoon mess. Viv flirts with a young man on her birthday. Bernice sacks Mandy from the pub

programme.desc: Angie discovers Mike paid Deirdre's defence bill out of the company account. Hayley comes into an inheritance from her father. Sally tells Kevin she is not ready for another baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy is depressed now Hayley has gone and ponders the meaning of love. Alma accuses Mike of having an affair. Zoe warns Judy that Shannon's health is none of her business

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish detective's silver wedding celebrations are interrupted by a fresh outbreak of crime on his patch. Five dead bodies have been found on board an abandoned lorry at the ferry terminal and a fugitive is on the run. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present two stories of women trying to bring their families back together. Mother and daughter Val and Marisa Moorhouse from West Yorkshire are united in their search for their missing son and brother Stephen, whom Val had adopted in 1977, when she felt she could not afford to give him the life he deserved. Meanwhile, 37-year-old Sam from Surrey is searching not only for her birth mother, but also her sister

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Elizabeth Bancroft is running an operation to against a vicious gang and adopts dubious methods to bring down the brothers who run it. Meanwhile, DS Katherine Stevens is assigned to a cold murder case and finds that there's more to it than it seems - and that Bancroft has some secrets in her past that may prove difficult to hide. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, with Faye Marsay, Art Malik and Amara Khan

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.desc: Mike warns Alma not to lecture him on the sanctity of marriage. Leanne is amazed to hear she has a half-brother. Ashley worries about Shannon coughing and wheezing

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A murder victim is found in unusual circumstances, buried with a cache of 100-year-old timber. A likely suspect is soon identified, only to be shot dead by a mystery assassin - a development that has repercussions for DI Lane's love life. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour Cornish copper, with Helen Masters

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When the remains of a teenager are found half-buried in a wood, Vera finds herself investigating a 30-year-old mystery. Identified as Carrie Telling, a girl who disappeared during the 1984 miners' strike, the team retrieves news footage from the protests that shows her taking pictures in the background. It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The murder of a caretaker has been so carefully planned and executed that the police are left without a single piece of evidence. Adding to the mystery is the fact that the crime is laid out in detail in a manual purchased three weeks earlier in the victim's name. Brooks and Casey eventually track down the killer, who confesses, but the trial is put in jeopardy by a canny defence lawyer. Guest starring Toby Stephens

programme.desc: Katherine and Joe's relationship progresses, while events take a grisly turn for the Kamara family - forcing Bancroft to offer Daanish a deal. programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider and Emily organise a demonstration outside the law courts. Angie rows with Mike. Zoe is fed up of having Shannon around her all the time. Les enjoys having his son around

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy enjoys looking after Shannon. Alma gives Mike an ultimatum. Deirdre is sent to see the prison psychiatrist. Mary Docherty approaches Ken with information about Jon

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Kersey and Lane investigate the death of a fisherman, who was killed while at sea, and find EC quotas are taking their toll in more ways than one. They also discover a new sense of rivalry in their working relationship, but a bit of healthy competition could give them the edge they need to solve the case. Jack Shepherd stars, with Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a Dutch au pair, which seems connected to rivalry amid the city's academics. His search leads him to a family estate in the Oxfordshire countryside where he meets three siblings divided by personal demons and shared loss. Endeavour suspects there is a link between the disappearance and an unsolved case from years before, but as the investigation continues, conflict erupts between his fellow officers. When a group of teenagers makes a horrifying discovery, he suspects someone is manipulating events from afar

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A murder inquiry is launched when the police are alerted to shocking internet footage showing a teenage girl being attacked and shot. The investigation is hindered by the lack of a body, but the video provides enough clues to eventually put a name to the victim and track down her killers. However, a vow of silence between the defendants means Jake and Alesha face one of their toughest court battles yet

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Bancroft is faced with a blast from the past that brings events from 1990 to the surface, and Katherine wrestles with new information that she is uncovering. programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A Russian sailor claims political asylum when his ship docks in Whitby, while Greengrass finds an unlikely partner in crime

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers where the money for his birthday party came from. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: There is trouble in the village when local children get their hands on some wartime ammunition, while Greengrass locks horns with Lord Ashfordly's new gamekeeper and Kate realises she is pregnant

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv and Kelly badger Roy about the honeymoon. Kathy gives a eulogy at Rachel's funeral. Eric is convinced that Graham is up to no good. Alan makes a decision about selling the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric explains his suspicions about Graham to Stella. Mandy urges Roy to sort out the honeymoon mess. Viv flirts with a young man on her birthday. Bernice sacks Mandy from the pub

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Angie discovers Mike paid Deirdre's defence bill out of the company account. Hayley comes into an inheritance from her father. Sally tells Kevin she is not ready for another baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy is depressed now Hayley has gone and ponders the meaning of love. Alma accuses Mike of having an affair. Zoe warns Judy that Shannon's health is none of her business

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish detective's silver wedding celebrations are interrupted by a fresh outbreak of crime on his patch. Five dead bodies have been found on board an abandoned lorry at the ferry terminal and a fugitive is on the run. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present two stories of women trying to bring their families back together. Mother and daughter Val and Marisa Moorhouse from West Yorkshire are united in their search for their missing son and brother Stephen, whom Val had adopted in 1977, when she felt she could not afford to give him the life he deserved. Meanwhile, 37-year-old Sam from Surrey is searching not only for her birth mother, but also her sister

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Elizabeth Bancroft is running an operation to against a vicious gang and adopts dubious methods to bring down the brothers who run it. Meanwhile, DS Katherine Stevens is assigned to a cold murder case and finds that there's more to it than it seems - and that Bancroft has some secrets in her past that may prove difficult to hide. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, with Faye Marsay, Art Malik and Amara Khan

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When the remains of a teenager are found half-buried in a wood, Vera finds herself investigating a 30-year-old mystery. Identified as Carrie Telling, a girl who disappeared during the 1984 miners' strike, the team retrieves news footage from the protests that shows her taking pictures in the background. It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The murder of a caretaker has been so carefully planned and executed that the police are left without a single piece of evidence. Adding to the mystery is the fact that the crime is laid out in detail in a manual purchased three weeks earlier in the victim's name. Brooks and Casey eventually track down the killer, who confesses, but the trial is put in jeopardy by a canny defence lawyer. Guest starring Toby Stephens

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Katherine and Joe's relationship progresses, while events take a grisly turn for the Kamara family - forcing Bancroft to offer Daanish a deal. programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Mysterious events unfold involving a beautiful blonde, and Templar gets behind the wheel of a rally car. Last-ever episode of the vintage adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Spikings finds his life in danger when he investigates the kidnapping of a police informant's son _ and uncovers corruption at the heart of SI-10. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle learn an assassin has been hired to murder one of the delegates at an anti-terrorist conference and set about discovering the identity of the killer's target. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry helps his friend Des by delivering a car, but it is a getaway vehicle and his fingerprints are found in it. When Terry is arrested and sent to court, Arthur has to act as a character witness, leaving his prospects of acquittal looking decidedly gloomy. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Neil Spooner and Tom Dove's mission to track down the world's largest carp takes them to Slovenia, visiting a remote lake full of submerged trees and a park lake that produced the country's record catch of almost 80lb

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Exasperated at the Sheriff's record in office, King John visits Sherwood to personally oversee the destruction of Robin and his outlaw band - a task he entrusts to the ruthless Roger de Carnac. Phil Davis and Matt Frewer guest star

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates a twisted tale of rock 'n' roll, meditation and murder. Anna Carteret and Trevor Bannister guest star, with Roger Moore

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo investigate when Harriet's father is the victim of a cunning burglar who sneaks in undetected and steals a priceless jade collection. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect Cowley's former girlfriend, now a controversial evangelist, from an assassin, but are unaware that the would-be hitman is right under their nose. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry are hired by two farmers to transport a prize bull, little suspecting they are actually being tricked into stealing it. When their error of judgement become apparent, the duo try to redeem themselves by working out a way to get the animal back. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner head to Italy and travel to a lake in the hills near Bologna, where they have just two days to catch a whopper. Then it is off for the main event at a large water called Viverone, not far from the Alps, where they go up against fish with a reputation for outwitting anglers

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Hannah Mills, Chris Ramsey, Dion Dublin and Lesley Garrett pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team is given two days and a $20,000 budget to remove a 10-metre steel-hulled yacht from Cockburn Beach in Western Australia. The problem is the boat is buried deep in the sand and is a danger to beach goers. Heavy machinery specialist Ryan is going to use his monster excavator to try and get the job done, but there's a catch - to make the budget work, Luke needs Ryan to be done and dusted in 24 hours.

programme.title: Bullitt (1968)
programme.desc: A tough police detective is assigned to protect a Mob witness for 48 hours, only for hitmen to find their hiding place and try to silence the informant. The rebellious cop realises something does not add up and goes in search of both the killers and whoever told them where to locate their target - but an ambitious politician wants him off the case. Thriller, starring Steve McQueen, Jacqueline Bisset, Robert Vaughn and Robert Duvall

programme.desc: The LAPD finds itself on the hunt for a thief from a local jewellery heist. Even though the case may look fairly pedestrian from the outset, Riggs and Murtaugh soon get a reminder that all that glitters is not gold and suddenly this straightforward looking case begins to evolve into something deeper. Murtaugh is erroneously called 'grandpa' by another parent while at the playground, sending him into a spiral of insecurity. Riggs battles some serious demons after vowing to withdraw from his drinking habit and work on his sobriety. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: News of a violent attack in a remote Mongolian river has Jeremy Wade wondering if it could be the work of a giant fish so rare that it's more legend than fact. But with illegal gold miners poisoning the last hideouts of this man-sized monster, it's anyone's guess whether the creature is still alive and biting

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Neil Spooner and Tom Dove travel to Thailand, where they have previously caught huge Siamese carp, but this time the focus is on the other species of carp found there. Their adventure is split between Fishing Park Samui and Gillhams Fishing Resorts, where they will have to use all of their skill and knowledge to outwit as many different carp species as possible, and in the process they will be hoping to bank one of the stunning Jullien's golden carp that can be found there

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Fern Britton, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard, lingerie tycoon Michelle Mone and actor Neil Morrissey pit their general knowledge against a trivia expert as they try to win thousands of pounds for charity. programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team is given two days and a $20,000 budget to remove a 10-metre steel-hulled yacht from Cockburn Beach in Western Australia. The problem is the boat is buried deep in the sand and is a danger to beach goers. Heavy machinery specialist Ryan is going to use his monster excavator to try and get the job done, but there's a catch - to make the budget work, Luke needs Ryan to be done and dusted in 24 hours.

programme.title: Bullitt (1968)
programme.desc: A tough police detective is assigned to protect a Mob witness for 48 hours, only for hitmen to find their hiding place and try to silence the informant. The rebellious cop realises something does not add up and goes in search of both the killers and whoever told them where to locate their target - but an ambitious politician wants him off the case. Thriller, starring Steve McQueen, Jacqueline Bisset, Robert Vaughn and Robert Duvall

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: The LAPD finds itself on the hunt for a thief from a local jewellery heist. Even though the case may look fairly pedestrian from the outset, Riggs and Murtaugh soon get a reminder that all that glitters is not gold and suddenly this straightforward looking case begins to evolve into something deeper. Murtaugh is erroneously called 'grandpa' by another parent while at the playground, sending him into a spiral of insecurity. Riggs battles some serious demons after vowing to withdraw from his drinking habit and work on his sobriety. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.desc: The Cranes take a trip to the mountains for a holiday away from the city, but Frasier, Niles and Daphne are plagued by nightmares during the overnight journey. Martin also has a strange dream, but unlike the rest of the family, continues to sleep soundly

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: New chateau owners Jayne and Steve need to get four en suite themed bedrooms ready for their new B&B business. For the Oriental suite, Jayne goes to visit Angel for help and advice. Plus, a chance to meet Brit Justin and his Aussie wife Annabelle, who are turning their chateau, with its sweeping views of the Pyrenees, into a family home

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three Cardiff couples compete to win £1,000 by throwing the perfect dinner part. First-time hosts Abi and Jake plan an American-themed menu. Their home-made fare impresses most of their guests, and their special effort for vegan couple Ashleigh and Jack is really appreciated. On night two, passionate vegans Ashleigh and Jack serve a fully plant-based menu designed to satisfy even the most committed meat eaters, but their rivals aren't convinced that they can be converted. On the final night, Karen and James are confident a more traditional menu will show the 'youngsters' how it's done

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix explores Mallorca's coast in a sailing boat, and meets a family who are attempting to launch a restaurant on the north of the island

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Professor Frink plays matchmaker when he creates an algorithm to make the lonely people of Springfield fall in love. When Marge, Bart, and Lisa visit Grampa for Valentine's Day, they find the old folks in a drug-induced hallucination that lets them relive happy memories

programme.title: Ackley Bridge
programme.desc: Kayla's world implodes when she hears where Fizza was on Friday. The school trip to the Brontë Parsonage Museum is a disaster, and Johnny attempts to cheer up Kayla, but his world is about to fall apart too

programme.desc: The McQueen family are forced to deal with the repercussions of recent events. Elsewhere, Darren asks Tony to abandon his matchmaking mission before anyone gets hurt

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: How to Save a Grand in 24 Hours
programme.desc: Anna Richardson and a team of cost-cutting experts offer viewers tips on saving money and sorting out their homes, beginning by helping a London family of six with their bills. Chef Gary Usher, DIY expert Eve Humphreys and cleaning wonder Peachy Clean find ways to cut their food bills and excessive shopping sprees and dish out some cleaning tips

programme.title: Baby Surgeons: Delivering Miracles
programme.desc: Doctors perform extraordinary surgery on babies still inside the womb, to save lives before they are born. This episode focuses on three expectant mothers navigating complex pregnancies. Becky's unborn baby has a rare lung tumour that is growing rapidly, Ann-Marie is carrying identical twins, one of which has selective growth restriction jeopardising both baby's chances of surviving, and Randika's dwarfism makes giving birth a difficult proposition

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the rising incidence of theft in Northamptonshire, where in 2018 burglaries hit a five-year high. PC Imogen Robinson chases a prolific shoplifter who has stolen a box of chocolate bars from a shop. PC Ben Norris is dispatched to apprehend a suspected burglar who then carries out a rooftop protest

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to a man who has had a seizure, but they are warned by the control room that the patient has a history of being abusive toward ambulance crews. In Bromsgrove, a paramedic team treat a man who has been coughing up blood and complaining of a loss of sensation in one leg. He may have a blood clot on his lungs which could stop him breathing

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Documentary following West Midlands Ambulance Service at the peak of the Covid-19 pandemic in April 2020, from the perspective of the crews, control room staff and managers as they deal with the human cost of the biggest ever challenge to the service. To get more ambulances onto the frontline, managers deploy student paramedics, with 21-year-old Sam and 20-year-old Caitlyn encountering patients with breathing difficulties, in care homes, and with mental health issues that appear isolation-related

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he meets a brother-and-sister team who find it almost impossible to work together. Their relationship is so fractured that they're failing to save their father's Italian restaurant Levanti's from being overwhelmed by local competition, and the situation has become so desperate that their dad is in danger of losing his life savings

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Birmingham compete for the £1,000 cash prize, with married couple Veda and Varun kicking off the competition with a Bollywood bash and an Indian-themed menu. programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: June negotiates a truce in the Waterfords' fractured relationship. Janine oversteps the mark with the Putnam family and a still-healing Aunt Lydia inflicts a brutal public punishment when protocol is broken. Dystopian drama continuing the story of Margaret Atwood's original novel, starring Elisabeth Moss and Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces

programme.desc: Sam lies to Diane about his plans for a weekend away, and Coach tries to break his record for fewest glasses smashed in a month, while Norm starts spending all his time with a new friend, leaving Cliff feeling neglected. American bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles buys an expensive sweater, despite promising Daphne he would start saving for their child's college fund, and Frasier agrees to get Roz and Alice tickets to see a show by his ex-wife, which only serves to fan the embers of lust. Laurie Metcalf guest stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin suffers a mild heart attack, but tries to keep it from Ronee - to the dismay of Frasier and Niles, who feel it would be unfair of him to hide such serious news. programme.title: Frasier

programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to help get the original chateau pizza oven working, while Annabelle and Justin return from holiday to find their chateau infested with bees

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.desc: Life aboard the most famous ocean liner in the world, now a floating hotel in Dubai. Bigger than Titanic, she transported rock stars and royalty and sailed over six million miles, circumnavigating the globe 25 times before being retired. With more than 100 five-star hotels, competition in the Dubai hotel market is fierce and though millions have already been spent on her refurbishment, she's still only 40 per cent complete. In this episode, the first VIP guests are due to check in and top chef Dino and his team have a big event planned - a traditional Burns night supper for 200 guests

programme.title: Dando: 20 Year Hunt for a Killer
programme.desc: Documentary re-examining the case of Jill Dando's murder, the BBC presenter who was mercilessly shot dead as she entered her Fulham home in April 1999. Two years after her death, Barry George was found guilty of her murder, but following a dramatic retrial in 2008, he was acquitted and released from prison. Now more than 20 years on, this programme looks back at some of the unanswered questions surrounding the case
