programme.title: Eight Days That Made Rome
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes focuses on events leading up to and after 9th June 68AD, when Emperor Nero took his life. She examines his relationship with his mother, fondness for debauchery and how casual violence and murder began to destabilise what had once been touted as a new 'Golden Age' for Rome. Nero's death plunged the empire into anarchy and civil war. From here on in, the Roman Empire would be plagued by military coups and revolt, one of the crucial factors in its eventual decline. Dramatisations featuring Adam Drew, Sarah-Jane Potts and Richard Brake bring the key moments to life

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Historian Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century visionary Dutch artist Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn, who was famed for his skilful handling of light and shade in paintings portraying the human form. Born in Leiden in 1606, the eighth of nine children, his passion for art led him to an apprenticeship with a local painter, and from the time he was a 19-year-old he had a studio in Amsterdam, where he produced 75 self-portraits. Demand for his talents soon grew and success came when he was commissioned to paint The Anatomy Lesson of Dr Tulp

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Writer and historian Dan Jones examines the creation of the picturesque Kennet and Avon Canal, which was born from a murky pool of dirty deals and demanding landowners. It came into being thanks to the genius of young engineer John Rennie, whose inspiration and innovations created a waterway that linked Bristol to London. Dan discovers how Rennie plotted the canal's route using the most basic of surveying instruments and marvels at the engineering innovations that made it all possible, such as the water-powered pumping station at Claverton

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The firearms team are deployed to the address of a man allegedly armed with a gun who is threatening to harm himself or provoke a police shooting. programme.title: Here Come the Gypsies!
programme.desc: In Hampshire, it's the day of the renowned Cooper horse drive, and hundreds of Gypsies gather to buy and sell horses and ride out together through the New Forest. programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. programme.desc: A psychological profile of the former nurse, who is currently serving 13 life sentences for murdering four children and injuring nine others at Grantham Hospital in Lincolnshire in 1991. programme.title: Eight Days That Made Rome
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes explores the day in 80AD when the Colosseum opened its gates for the first time. For new emperor Titus, the spectacular games and events were an opportunity to win over the people and secure his place on the imperial throne, but why did the Romans - cultured and civilised in so many ways - enjoy witnessing such brutality and bloodletting? Bettany travels across the Roman world in a bid to find answers. Dramatisations featuring Sam Barriscale, Oliver Monaghan and Paul Lacoux bring the key moments to life

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Seventeenth-century Dutch painter Jan Vermeer, the lowly son of an innkeeper and art dealer, lived an uneventful life, leaving fewer than 40 authenticated works and dying unknown outside his home town. Tim Marlow investigates how his talent came to be regarded as highly as Rembrandt's, and examines the simple domestic scenes that led to his posthumous rise to fame

programme.desc: PC Dave Naylor responds to a call after a man with a history of violence is suspected of having stolen a car, which is pursued at 95mph along the M1, while police receive information about a man suspected of trying to sell a car for £1,000 and then driving off with both the vehicle and the cash

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Security is called after a patient suspected of having Covid-19 ties wires around his door handles in protest after being asked not to roam around the department, while 25-year-old Chelsea arrives in resus by air ambulance after being hit by a car while on a night out. In minors, 68-year-old Roger has managed to accidentally swallow disinfectant and is worried he's caused serious internal damage, while a team tries to revive a patient who is having a cardiac arrest

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Doctors in the accident and emergency department treat a patient in cardiac arrest. Junior doctor George Kay leads the resus team, while Hannah Ryan steps in to head up the nurses. Meanwhile, cyclist Garry is rushed into hospital after a head-on collision with a metal gate, and elderly patient Larry comes to hospital with his wife Molly after he had a fall at home

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Critical care paramedic Pete Edwards and the Midlands Air Ambulance Trauma Team are dispatched to give lifesaving treatment to a 21-year-old motorcyclist who has been thrown off his bike, with his helmet also coming off in the impact. Elsewhere, Rob Davis battles to save the life of an 80-year-old who is in cardiac arrest on his driveway, and the team is called to a man who has collapsed with a suspected heart attack and is showing symptoms of Covid-19

programme.title: Extraordinary People
programme.desc: In 2010, US marine sergeant John Peck lost his arms and legs while serving in Afghanistan. Dissatisfied with his prosthetics and determined to regain his independence, he put himself forward for ground-breaking surgery. This documentary follows John as he undergoes a bilateral arm transplant in an extraordinary 10-hour operation with 20 surgical staff in attendance

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, and the subject of children proves a thorny one for Monica and Richard. Rachel relives the ordeal of her failed wedding day while serving as bridesmaid at her former fiance's ceremony. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross foolishly lets Rachel in on his most secret sexual fantasy, while Phoebe consoles a heartbroken Monica and Chandler gets involved with an old flame whom Joey cannot stand. David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston star

programme.title: Eight Days That Made Rome
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes focuses on the day when Roman troops earned the undying hatred of a fierce and fearless queen who led a revolt that came perilously close to ending the Roman occupation of Britannia. Around 60AD troops invaded Boudica's settlement, flogged her and raped her daughters. The outrage provoked the Iceni queen to lead a revolt that came perilously close to ending the Roman occupation of Britannia. Dramatisations featuring Aislinn Sands, Ross O'Hennessy and Nicholas Lopez bring the key moments to life

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow explores the life and work of ground-breaking artist Diego de Silva y Velazquez, whose paintings provide an insight into the development of the Spanish monarchy in the 17th century - and the decline of the most powerful royal court in Europe

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Compilation of highlights from the home improvement series, featuring bedroom and bathroom makeovers by Californian designer Ann Maurice

programme.title: The Wrecking Crew (1968)
programme.desc: A secret agent sets out to recover gold bullion stolen from a hijacked train being taken across America by cunning thieves. Fourth and final film in the Matt Helm comedy spy series, with Dean Martin, Sharon Tate, Elke Sommer, Nancy Kwan and Tina Louise

programme.title: Destroyer (1943)
programme.desc: A former welder working on the Navy destroyer whose construction he oversaw angers his crewmates with his relentless quest for perfection. Wartime adventure, starring Edward G Robinson, Glenn Ford, Marguerite Chapman and Regis Toomey

programme.title: Hell Is for Heroes (1962)
programme.desc: A rebellious soldier defies orders to lead two men on a secret attack on a German pillbox, but his mission fails and the other two soldiers are killed. Court-martialled for his actions, he remains convinced his plan was sound and ignores the sentence to make a second attempt at destroying the emplacement. Second World War adventure, with Steve McQueen, James Coburn and Bobby Darin

programme.title: Gunsmoke (1953)
programme.desc: A wicked landowner wants to get his hands on a farmer's ranch, so hires a gunslinger to kill him. However, the mercenary befriends his intended target - and also falls for his daughter - so resolves to defend them from their enemies during a cattle drive. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Paul Kelly, Susan Cabot and Donald Randolph

programme.title: City Slickers (1991)
programme.desc: Three middle-aged men take a break from the rat race and their mid-life crises to join a two-week cattle drive from New Mexico to Colorado. Together with a bunch of fellow urbanites, they find the going much tougher than expected, and it is down to a real-life cowboy to whip them into shape. Comedy adventure, starring Billy Crystal, Daniel Stern, Bruno Kirby and Jack Palance, who won an Oscar for his performance as grizzled trail boss Curly

programme.title: The Kid (2019)
programme.desc: In the Wild West of the 19th century, a youngster named Rio Cutler goes on the run with his sister Sara to escape their violent uncle Grant. Along the way, they meet Billy the Kid and his gang, forming an unlikely alliance with the infamous outlaw. Western adventure directed by Vincent D'Onofrio, starring Chris Pratt, Ethan Hawke, Dane DeHaan and Jake Schur

programme.title: True Grit (2010)
programme.desc: A farmer is murdered by a hired hand, who flees into the wilderness. The dead man's strong-willed 14-year-old daughter is determined to bring the killer to justice, and recruits the aid of a hard-drinking, one-eyed marshal to help her track him down. The Coen brothers' Western based on Charles Portis' novel, with Jeff Bridges, Hailee Steinfeld, Matt Damon and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Benjamin (2018)
programme.desc: An up-and-coming filmmaker tries to balance a romance with a visiting French music student and the release of his second movie. A lot of time has elapsed since his acclaimed debut and Benjamin is concerned that his follow-up will be a disaster. Writer/director Simon Amstell's comedy drama, starring Colin Morgan, Jack Rowan and Anna Chancellor, with cameos from Mark Kermode and Simon Mayo

programme.title: Cottage to Let (1941)
programme.desc: A British inventor's revolutionary bombsight is set to have a positive impact on the war effort. However, military intelligence detects the Nazis are aware of the invention, while a plucky teenage cockney evacuated to the boffin's Scottish country estate sets out to expose a German spy ring. Second World War thriller, with Leslie Banks, John Mills, Alastair Sim and George Cole in his screen debut

programme.title: The Last Blitzkrieg (1959)
programme.desc: The son of a Nazi general is selected for a secret undercover mission. He and his men disguise themselves as American soldiers and go behind the Allied lines, with the intention of carrying out acts of sabotage and undermining enemy morale. Second World War drama, starring Van Johnson and Kerwin Mathews

programme.title: Last Train from Gun Hill (1959)
programme.desc: A marshal discovers the man responsible for raping and killing his wife is actually the son of an old friend, setting the scene for a showdown. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Anthony Quinn, Brian Hutton and Carolyn Jones

programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther (1975)
programme.desc: The inept Inspector Clouseau sets out to unmask a notorious international jewel thief believed to be behind the theft of a priceless diamond - but anarchy, as ever, follows wherever he goes. Blake Edwards' comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Christopher Plummer, Catherine Schell, Herbert Lom, Burt Kwouk and Graham Stark

programme.title: Warcraft (2016)
programme.desc: A kingdom stands on the brink of war when it is invaded by a vast horde of creatures who have travelled through a portal from their dying world. As dark magic works its influence on the world, two leaders from either side are set on a collision course with each other as they strive to protect their people. Fantasy adventure based on the best-selling computer game, starring Travis Fimmel, Paula Patton and Ben Foster

programme.title: The Drop (2014)
programme.desc: A Brooklyn bartender secretly provides a service delivering shipments of money to local gangsters. His criminal connections lead to his involvement in a robbery that goes disastrously wrong and digs up the long-buried secrets from his neighbourhood's past. Crime drama based on a short story by Dennis Lehane, starring Tom Hardy, Noomi Rapace and James Gandolfini in his final film role

programme.title: The Crying Game (1992)
programme.desc: An IRA member holds a British soldier hostage. The pair form an unlikely bond - so when the situation ends in tragedy, the guilt-ridden terrorist sets out to find his former prisoner's lover. But romance is soon on the cards, with devastating results. Neil Jordan's thriller, starring Stephen Rea, Jaye Davidson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Gemma Bovery (2014)
programme.desc: An Englishwoman goes to live in the French village that was the setting for Flaubert's novel Madame Bovary. As her marriage begins to fall apart, she begins to see parallels between herself and the book's heroine, and finds herself at a crossroads in her life when an old flame reappears. Comedy drama based on the graphic novel by Posy Simmonds, starring Gemma Arterton and Fabrice Luchini

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Bonnie and Danny take a cookery class run by a noted chef, but when Danny wins the chance to be a guest sous chef, Bonnie is determined to prove that she's the better cook. Tucker advises Riley to make up a fake boyfriend in order to fix her friendship with Danny

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben and Riley play a game of 'shot and score', where they compete to see who can kiss a stranger first. Riley sets her sights on a handsome bar patron, while Ben hits it off with another customer. However, when Riley is horrified when her one-night stand turns out to be her new boss. Bonnie uses Riley's office for her real estate business, and Danny makes a big investment in his future

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Advertising executive Andre 'Dre''Johnson is happily anticipating a promotion that would make him his company's first black senior vice-president, while his wife Rainbow, an anaesthetist, is looking forward to their successful future. Pilot of the comedy following the lives of an upper-middle-class African-American family, starring Anthony Anderson, Tracee Ellis Ross and Laurence Fishburne.

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre is upset to find out that Rainbow has already told Andre Jr about adulthood because she thinks he's too much of a prude to do it. However, he soon regrets giving his son his own version of 'the talk' when Andre Jr starts to drive him mad with non-stop questions about sex. Comedy, starring Anthony Anderson

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel begins a relationship with a younger man, while Lennox faces a challenge from one of her classmates when she takes up the post of editor of the school blog. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.desc: The Taskmaster Greg Davies sends fear coursing through the veins of James Acaster, Jessica Knappett, Kerry Godliman, Phil Wang and Rhod Gilbert. In this edition, the challenges include an attempt to cheer up a grumpy traffic warden

programme.desc: A bobsledder for the British Olympic team enjoys life as a bachelor and is uncertain about starting a relationship - but a date with a kickboxer and fellow Olympian might just change his mind. Two other men arrive for their dates with very specific ideas of what they want, leaving the women they meet uncertain whether they want to take the relationship further

programme.title: Lego Masters USA
programme.desc: The contestants are given an everyday object, such as a bicycle or radio, that has been sliced in two, and they are tasked with building its other half out of Lego

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Tension mounts when the pals attend a weekend science conference. Howard becomes argumentative after Bernadette runs into a former boyfriend, and the fallout causes chaos with the hotel room arrangements. Rick Fox guest stars, with Simon Helberg and Melissa Rauch

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon agrees to participate in a series of short lectures at the university, but is worried the students dislike him. Following Amy's advice, he asks Penny to give him acting lessons to help him engage with his audience. Elsewhere, Raj fantasises about his best friend's partner. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Andi hires a young babysitter, only to get jealous when daughter Kate goes to her for advice. Adam, meanwhile, gets a surprise when the babysitter admits she finds him attractive

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: When Adam discovers the pastor he hired for their wedding was a phony and he and Andi are not really married, he scrambles to find a way to fix it before she finds out

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben takes Emma to his old doctor for a check-up and admits he hasn't had a medical for years. When the physician insists Ben has an examination and finds something of concern, Ben has a panic attack about his future and worries about who will take care of Emma if he's no longer around. Bonnie has lunch with Brad, having mistaken him for his twin brother Tad. Comedy, with Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley realises that she hasn't met any of Ross's friends and decides to throw a cocktail party so she can get acquainted with them. Danny is worried that he knows nothing about his new girlfriend Ashley and goes to great lengths to find out her last name. Bonnie has some serious explaining to do when a valuable item goes missing. Eddie Cibrian guest stars

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Although Dre and Rainbow have agreed that they will no longer use corporal punishment to discipline the children, all bets might be off when Jack behaves disobediently. Zoey, Andre Jr, Diane and a nervous Jack wait to find out whether their parents will go back on their word. Comedy, starring Anthony Anderson, Tracee Ellis Ross and Laurence Fishburne

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre fears his fun will be ruined when the older kids lose their enthusiasm for the Johnson family Halloween tradition of seeing who can pull the most outlandish prank

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe's younger half-brother Tony visits and Mel is so impressed by his photography work that she asks him to take some pictures of her. Meanwhile, Lennox tries to avoid an unwell Ryder so she does not succumb to illness before a big party at the weekend. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe gets Ryder into trouble at school when he helps him complete a homework assignment. Meanwhile, Mel sneaks Lennox into a concert she is not old enough to attend. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is forced to consider how far he is willing to push himself in the name of science when a wealthy benefactor makes an indecent proposal. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, with Jessica Walter (Arrested Development) and Joshua Malina (The West Wing)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard is torn between his relationship with Bernadette and pleasing his mother - and comes up with a solution that leaves everyone unsatisfied. Leonard rekindles his romance with Raj's sister Priya, while Amy uses Penny's emotions as the basis for a research project

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam tries to start a Mel Brooks club after he sees the movie Spaceballs, but Jackie mistakes his club advertisement for political satire and puts it in the school paper. When Beverly and Pops come up for parents' weekend at college, Erica plans to tell them about an important decision she has made, but Murray surprises everyone by tagging along

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Lainey moves back to town and Barry invites her to the prom, but when she refuses, he plans a prank with Principal Ball's car to get out of going. Erica tells Murray she has made a final decision about her future at college, but he is so furious that he insists she pay her own way for rent and food

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta offers a drug case to Scully and Hitchcock because he wants to work on a case with Boyle that Holt says involves a celebrity, while Amy and Gina learn that Rosa never attends blood-donation drives because she is afraid of needles

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The precinct gets a new detective who is coming away from a 12-year undercover assignment with the Mob. Boyle's latest mess lands everyone in trouble with the caretakers, and Gina directs a video about the precinct for Captain Holt. Jason Mantzoukas and Kate Flannery guest star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon stumbles upon a major scientific breakthrough, causing him to feel like a fraud, while Penny steps up her game with Leonard after seeing Howard make a romantic gesture to Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is hurt when Professor Proton (Bob Newark) contacts Leonard for help, so he befriends TV scientist Bill Nye, and Raj gets jealous when Howard crashes a girls' night in making home-made jewellery. Starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki, and Kunal Nayyar

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: When Mel punches a man who picks on Ryder at the cinema, a clip of the fracas is posted online and she becomes known as 'Battling Burke', much to the delight of her political campaign manager. Meanwhile, Lennox befriends a new girl at school, but she soon takes advantage of his kindness. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel wants to break up with her boyfriend Donald, but she feels guilty because he has just been fired from his job - so she asks Joe to help boost Donald's confidence before she dumps him. Meanwhile, Holly confides a secret to Lennox, and asks her to keep it from Ryder

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry thinks he wants a wild, adult bachelor party, but he soon realises Adam's idea of a soiree to celebrate his brother as a bachelor is not to his liking. Murray never spends money on himself, so Beverly decides to surprise him with an expensive sports car

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry is thrilled that he gets to celebrate Christmas now that he is engaged to Lainey. Meanwhile, Murray's brother Marvin makes a promise to Adam with a surprising outcome

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When Andre Jr is bullied at school, Dre teaches his son how to defend himself by 'trash talking' his opponent into submission - but his lessons work too well. Rainbow struggles with sleep deprivation when Diane develops a fear of the dark

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre organises an amazing vow renewal ceremony for his and Rainbow's 15th anniversary to make up for the no-frills, last-minute wedding they had. However, when Rainbow's parents Alicia and Paul turn up unannounced, bombshells are dropped and old disagreements re-ignite

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Part three of three. The team at the precinct go on a road trip to help Peralta and Holt take down crime boss Jimmy 'The Butcher' Figgis, but the plan is hindered by an unforeseen injury and an interruption by Sheriff Reynolds. In the middle of the chaos, Santiago and Peralta share an awkward reunion

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: In a New Girl crossover episode, Peralta runs into Jess Day, who is visiting New York from Los Angeles, and enlists her help on his latest case. Holt looks for ways to improve the team's morale as they come to terms with being on the night shift, while Santiago is filled with curiosity when Diaz starts to take long breaks from her desk. Zooey Deschanel (New Girl) and Matt Walsh (Veep) guest star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard's purchase of a dining-room table causes Sheldon to re-evaluate his life. Meanwhile, Howard is offered the chance to go back to the International Space Station, and Bernadette struggles to decide whether to encourage him. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Melissa Rauch

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After arguing with Sheldon, Howard tries to make amends by taking him to Nasa's headquarters in Houston. Elsewhere, Penny has second thoughts about quitting her job as a waitress when her car dies, while Amy tries to find Raj a date. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Gogglesprogs

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: On a weekend cabin trip with Penny and Amy, Sheldon spills one of Leonard's secrets, while Howard and Bernadette must draw a line when Raj goes overboard with pregnancy gifts. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal and his mother Linda, Denise van Outen and her boyfriend Eddie Boxshall, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, Stanley Johnson and Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo, Oti Mabuse and her sisters Motsie and Phemelo, Emily Atack and Kate Robbins, Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock, and Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv critique the week's TV. Shows including including American Pie, The Graham Norton Show, Love Island, Natural World - Walrus: Two Tonne Tusker, Killing Eve, Blind Date, Jeremy Wade's Dark Waters, and The Family Brain Game come under their gaze. First shown in June 2019

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. They then they whittle the candidates down until only one remains. In the first edition, Aina, a music producer from London, wants to find someone adventurous who she can have a deeper connection with, while Guildford-based designer Mal - who has dated men and women - is curious to see who she will be drawn to when all options are laid bare before her

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: While the girls go to Disneyland and get made up as princesses, the guys have to promote science as a career option for women at Howard's old middle school. Meanwhile, Raj and Lucy have their first date in a library, where instead of speaking to each other they send text messages

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Bernadette and Howard host a dinner party, but as it falls on Sheldon's Thai food night, he opts to bring his own dinner. As punishment for his lack of manners, the gang decides he should organise Wolowitz's cluttered wardrobe, but he discovers an unopened letter from Howard's father, who hasn't made contact in years

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, including Strictly Come Dancing, Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railways, Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie, Sheridan, Nigella: At My Table and the News. The programme captures their instant reactions and lively discussions from the comfort of their own homes. From November 2017

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Jessica Knappett beautifully mimics a foghorn, Phil Wang makes ogres poignant despite his eye-catching costume and James Acaster finds various circles

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: The singletons include 64-year-old self-confessed cougar Lynda, who has now decided she wants to find a more mature man who shares her lust for life. Thirty-eight-year-old meat-loving teacher Marie has to keep her sledgehammer wit in check when she finds out that her date, fellow teacher Davin, is a vegan, and following one of the most excruciating dates in First Dates history, Fred is determined to find 25-year-old DJ Tom love

They then they whittle the candidates down until only one remains. In the first edition, Aina, a music producer from London, wants to find someone adventurous who she can have a deeper connection with, while Guildford-based designer Mal - who has dated men and women - is curious to see who she will be drawn to when all options are laid bare before her

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard is torn between his relationship with Bernadette and pleasing his mother - and comes up with a solution that leaves everyone unsatisfied. Leonard rekindles his romance with Raj's sister Priya, while Amy uses Penny's emotions as the basis for a research project

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi decide to go on strike when the kids refuse to do their chores. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam needs to hire a new project manager, and Andi accuses him of unconscious bias after he only interviews men. Andi helps Kate fight for a spot on the boys' soccer team

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Amber and Colin turn their bedroom into a dream vintage haven. After an initial assessment, the presenter begins by getting rid of any mass-produced furniture

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: After a long-term relationship came to an end and her son flew the nest, Alix Sinclare sold her family home in Devon. She's now looking for the perfect place to start a new life but cannot decide between current home in Teignmouth, Devon, and Nerja on Spain's Costa del Sol. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin offer advice, with Jonnie anxious to convince her that Spain is the place to be

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon O'Brien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien finds a barn containing a collection of replica Rolls-Royce cars, and Henry Cole tries his hand at driving a tractor as they continue their money-making tour

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Return of the programme following the work of probate detectives as they try to find people who are unknowingly heirs, sometimes to substantial fortunes. In the first edition, the researchers unearth the history of Croydon's perfume industry as they trace the relatives of a woman who died in the seaside town of Poole, Dorset. Plus, two estranged brothers, who grew up unaware of the other's existence, meet for the first time

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following British expats as they chase a dream life in the sun and set up new businesses in Spain and France. Steve Stallion plans to open a bar on the Costa Blanca with his mother-in-law, a couple of total novices leave Scotland to run a B&B in the southwest of France, and fishing-boat charter boss Dean Hoyle hands over his beloved Costa del Sol business to a new owner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Ian and Sam Sugden's Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, where the guests are impressed with the cleanliness of the rooms. However, there is some grumbling over breakfast about the bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners stay at Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, which is run by Lisa Holloway - who prides herself on providing a home away from home for her visitors. However, some of the guests have issues with dust and the haddock served at breakfast - and challenge whether a night's stay is worth the price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Jo Bate welcome their guests to the White Horse, a pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, where they hope an afternoon of morris dancing in front of the cathedral will make a lasting impression. Unfortunately, Ed and Jane Mitchell get locked inside their room by mistake, and there are arguments over tomatoes at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell welcome the contestants to the Old Farmhouse, Cheshire, but everyone is shocked to discover they will have to share a bathroom. Meanwhile, Sam and Ian Sugden find an unwelcome surprise underneath their bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day arrives, as the guests gather to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences - from some fishy findings to a reminder of why sorry can be the hardest word. Later on, a heated exchange about cleaning ends in tears

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives find themselves under pressure to trace the relatives of a former RAF officer, but the task proves difficult because he has a common surname. Plus, the story of a man who, having already lost two brothers in the First World War, suffered further tragedy while protecting his family decades later during the Blitz

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Mr Parsons the chameleon meets a new partner, Daphne from Paris. Jaguars Napo and Goshi are being encouraged to mate, but Nap doesn't seem to be romantically inclined. Young giraffe Murchison faces the great outdoors for the first time, and another baby elephant is born, less than a month after the last new arrival

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of musician Toby Leeming as he sets out to build a house wrapped around an ancient oak tree in rural Suffolk. The design for Toby's five-building home contains six bedrooms, six bathrooms, a music studio and an annexe - a vast project more like a village than a house. Creating this life changing vision on a £530k budget, and in only eight months, will not be an easy task, especially as they must build up close to an ancient oak without destroying any of the roots

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The stories behind sunken vessels around the world. The first episode features the exploration of two Scottish wrecks, HMS Hampshire and HMS Vanguard

programme.title: Hunt for the Arctic Ghost Ship
programme.desc: Cameras follow the multimillion-dollar expedition that discovered the Victorian ship HMS Erebus, intact and upright on the Arctic sea floor in September 2014. The story unfolds in the freezing Arctic, where search vessels battled with heavy sea-ice that threatened to stop the search entirely, to the dramatic chain of events that led to the historic discovery

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of the best bits from recent series with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley, joined by a host of top comedians including Alan Carr, Richard Ayoade, Kathy Burke, Roisin Conaty, Joe Wilkinson, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Ryan, Phil Wang, Aisling Bea and David Mitchell

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The stories behind sunken vessels around the world. The first episode features the exploration of two Scottish wrecks, HMS Hampshire and HMS Vanguard

programme.title: Hunt for the Arctic Ghost Ship
programme.desc: Cameras follow the multimillion-dollar expedition that discovered the Victorian ship HMS Erebus, intact and upright on the Arctic sea floor in September 2014. The story unfolds in the freezing Arctic, where search vessels battled with heavy sea-ice that threatened to stop the search entirely, to the dramatic chain of events that led to the historic discovery

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Chile to look into coloured crisps and find out how these rainbow snacks get their colour. Jimmy Doherty travels to Spain to investigate one of the trendiest items on the shelves, avocados, and the rapidly rising demand for these fruits which is leading to alarming reports of a crime wave hitting growers

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp introduces new crafts, skills and techniques for interior decoration. In this edition, she reveals how to create a personalised vintage doormat and coasters, using spray paint, old tiles and serviettes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Recently retired couple Nick and Jane Purser are looking forward to settling down, but cannot decide where that should be. Jane's mother lives in Liverpool, so she would prefer to stay close by with a move to Lancaster. Nick wants them to move to southern France, where he can ride his motorbike in the sun. Jasmine Harman shows the pair three properties in Lancaster for up to £180,000 and Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three properties for up to £145,000 in France

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers try to track down the next of kin of a Newcastle man with a £100,000 estate who failed to leave a will, while another search unveils an intriguing story about London chimney sweeps in the early 20th century. Plus, details of unclaimed estates that are still in need of heirs

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A family race to open their dream bar, two new B&B owners welcome their first guests in the French Dordogne, and can the newest fisherman on the Costa del Sol impress his first customers?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man, where owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace their green ethos. They kick things off with an afternoon of laser mayhem, and continue the fun the next day by following breakfast with hands-on animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Arches on the Isle of Man for their second stay, where owner Phil Routledge hopes to wow them with scenic views and scuba diving at the B&B's own pool. However, some of the owners are dismayed by modern gadgets with a lack of instructions, and even find cobwebs in their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants arrive at the Trafford in Blackpool, owned by Robbie and John Welling, who are proud of their traditional 'bucket and spade' B&B. The guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is intent on finding dust as a bit of tit-for-tat retaliation, and the afternoon is spent learning the art of making sticks of rock

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Host Julie welcomes the contestants to Sheepy Lodge, Warwickshire, where they are shocked by their surroundings, and disappointed by a variety of things in the rooms. The host puts on some old-fashioned parlour games, before a fierce hide-and-seek contest erupts. At breakfast, Julie frets when she must prepare poached eggs, and is left emotional after the feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival B&B owners meet to settle some scores and discover what they have been paid. Knockaloe Beg Farm owners Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms of a missing host at breakfast, and Phil Routledge from The Arches on the Isle of Man takes on Robbie and John Welling from The Trafford in Blackpool about their 'forensic investigation'. After some heated discussion, the winner is announced

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers look for beneficiaries to the £750,000 estate of an Oxfordshire man who died in 2011, and try to find out if a Salford man who they have been tracing relatives for owned his own property. Plus, details of unclaimed estates with heirs still waiting to be found

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Black rhino Kitani is due to give birth at Chester Zoo and help swell the ranks of this endangered species, but, having lost three previous calves, it is a worrying time and the keepers are concerned that things aren't going to plan. The zoo also has four rare new arrivals called tenrecs, mysterious mammals from Madagascar and, with so little known about them, the keepers try everything they can to help them breed, while red river hog Mali has given birth to twins, but tearaway dad Confetti is a threat to his own babies

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim battles to get a fence built for their marauding flock of 25 sheep, and Krys battles to balance the books. They race to get their chateau ready for their first paying guests as Lee struggles with a chandelier. Elaine and David continue the search for their dream property, visiting a 17th-century ex-priory in Normandy and taking on board all that Dick has taught them

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: For most people, living in a lighthouse is pure fantasy. Not so for Edward and Hazel Short and their two young daughters. Back in 2012, they set out to build a brand new, art deco, shining white lighthouse tower with additional bedrooms, guest rooms, and infinity pool on the side - all on a rugged but beautiful clifftop in North Devon. Kevin McCloud sees if they made that dream a reality

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw travel to Essex to pick up a real 1970s supercar - a Lotus Esprit S1 - a car made famous when it transformed into a submarine in the James Bond movie The Spy Who Loved Me

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The Morgan Motor Company provides unrestricted access to the factory floors, opening its doors to reveal how its £90,000 Plus Six sports car is made, with 170 craftspeople using classic coach-building techniques

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan nudges Jimmy Carr out of the hosting chair, inviting Roisin Conaty and Jessica Hynes to take on Sara Pascoe and Lolly Adefope in an all-female edition, originally shown to mark 100 years of women's suffrage. Rachel Riley is at the letters and numbers board, while Morgana Robinson appears in character as EastEnders' Natalie Cassidy alongside wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow couples as they cope with treatment, including Corey, who's 27. He is rushed to St George's after suffering a cardiac arrest during a game of ice hockey, and his wife of seven days, Alice, was watching him play when he collapsed. There's also a profile of John, 55. He fell 10 feet while taking down scaffolding at home, and his spouse Penny talks about how he has supported her through sickness and bereavement during their marriage

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw travel to Essex to pick up a real 1970s supercar - a Lotus Esprit S1 - a car made famous when it transformed into a submarine in the James Bond movie The Spy Who Loved Me

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The Morgan Motor Company provides unrestricted access to the factory floors, opening its doors to reveal how its £90,000 Plus Six sports car is made, with 170 craftspeople using classic coach-building techniques

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads on the trail of an elusive fruit - the fresh date, visiting a vast water purification plant in Israel where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is harnessed to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton flies to California to investigate why almond butter is more expensive than other nutty spreads, where she discovers a combination of extreme weather and global economics have affected the price of almonds. Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the true origins of haggis

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enlists the help of paper-cutting expert Poppy Chancellor to make personalised wedding invitations for a couple about to tie the knot. Plus, the inventive presenter creates a craft collage to celebrate the arrival of a newborn baby

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: When her partner of 20 years died, Ginny Lunn decided to find a holiday home where she could create new memories. Torn between buying a place in Dorset within easy reach of her London home for a budget of £250,000, and the Costa Tropical in Spain, where she would have a budget of £150,000, she requires help. Jasmine Harman shows her three options in Dorset, and Laura Hamilton tries to impress her with three properties in Spain, but, with the help of her sister Sara, which one will Ginny choose?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers are asked to track down the relatives of a Lancashire farmer who died without leaving a will. Plus, the story of a search for heirs that involved two generations of women who had played invaluable roles on Britain's production lines

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two French B&B hosts receive their first customer review. One expat fisherman has a big catch in Cape Verde, while another comes back empty-handed in Spain, and a boutique Spanish hotel launches a wedding business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton, Merseyside, where owner Alan Johnson and his professional sidekick Dave Owen hope to win the title - but a scandal with some unwanted hair on the bed sheets is not a good start, and two guests feel they are making an exhibition of themselves with a tea-trolley in full view of the neighbours

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the second day of the competition, the contestants visit the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley in Lancashire, and owners Nick and Gina Mead hope to impress them with an afternoon of entertainment out on the locks. At breakfast, one of the guests admits to not enjoying his stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire, where Brock Bergius hopes that his cheeky sense of humour and perfectionist nature will help him win the competition. He is in his element making saucy jokes during an afternoon of sausage making, but his reluctance to stray from the menu at breakfast leaves some of his guests disappointed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Heyford House in Oxfordshire, where Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little hope to impress the contestants with their hospitality. The afternoon event is boxing, which Brock Burgius sees as the perfect opportunity to settle some scores with his fellow competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's winner is announced as the B&B owners meet for the last time to thrash out their differences. Feedback includes criticism of a badly positioned tea tray, controversy over a hot chocolate and accusations of overenthusiastic hosting

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives visit a stately home in Warwickshire as they try to find relatives of a lady who was born there, and an heir on another case embarks on a journey of discovery to learn about the grandfather he never met. Plus, details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers try to work out why male red panda Jung is losing weight, with a full medical examination scheduled after a special diet fails to solve the problem. West African mandrill JC is being challenged for his dominant position by his sons Ludo and Jareth and, while JC is taken for some health checks, Ludo gets 'over-friendly' with his dad's girlfriend. Tempers start to fray among Chester Zoo's Humboldt penguin colony during the annual moult - can youngsters Frazzle and Munch avoid being picked on by the grown-ups?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Belinda travels to Dick and Angel's chateau so that Angel can teach her the art of decoupage on a classic 1950s family dressing table. It's changeover day at Krys and Tim's chateau, as they sweat it out in 42-degree heat to get their 20-room chateau ready for their next booking. Budding chateau owners Elaine and David visit a 19th-century chateau with 18 rooms and two gîtes in Normandy

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a home built on an airfield in Strathaven in Scotland to check on the progress made by Colin MacKinnon and his partner Marta Briongos. Their ambitious plan was to build a metal-sculptural home inspired by aircraft hangars right next to the runway, but the difficulties of building their elegant design saw a year-long project stretch to three years

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps more people as they build affordable dream homes. He begins in Hertfordshire with retired firefighter Bob and his wife Wendy, who spent years looking for the perfect building plot - until they found it in their back garden. They want somewhere to play music, paint and write, but they soon find themselves facing an ever-expanding budget to finish the house

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows more individuals Down Under as they design and construct a new home. He begins with Rob and Sally, who have bought the land for their new house in Mystery Bay, New South Wales, but soon face endless challenges - the building must withstand the elements, celebrate the amazing views and blend effortlessly into the surroundings.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Michelle, who's 54, is airlifted to St George's after being involved in a car crash with her husband Paul in Surrey. She was given a blood transfusion at the scene and doctors work quickly to get her to CT to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. Cameras also follow Derren, 41, who has fractured his ankle while trampolining with his 12-year-old daughter, Macie

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. Susie Dent is joined by Spencer Jones in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps more people as they build affordable dream homes. He begins in Hertfordshire with retired firefighter Bob and his wife Wendy, who spent years looking for the perfect building plot - until they found it in their back garden. They want somewhere to play music, paint and write, but they soon find themselves facing an ever-expanding budget to finish the house

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows more individuals Down Under as they design and construct a new home. He begins with Rob and Sally, who have bought the land for their new house in Mystery Bay, New South Wales, but soon face endless challenges - the building must withstand the elements, celebrate the amazing views and blend effortlessly into the surroundings.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team members select some of their favourite investigations from the show. Putting plums, prunes and prune juice to the test, Jimmy Doherty heads to King's College London to make a concoction that will help keep people's bowel movements regular. Kate Quilton visits Vietnam to learn about the difference between black and white pepper, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves, while Matt Tebbutt is in Switzerland to find out why there are holes in Swiss cheese

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: When the only trail groomer in town breaks down on the 'snowmobile highway', the boys are called out to do some trail-side repairs

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: The CRB crew helps a local entrepreneur get into the paving business by building an asphalt recycling truck from scratch

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Featuring a Texas attorney, who has a second life as a super picker, and possesses a wild warehouse of eclectic things

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Nat and John's day in Glasgow gets off to a flying start, but Dan's dirty tactics lead to mayhem within the team. Meanwhile, George finds himself with a new pet

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: The bidders head to Preston, where Dan's arrogant behaviour gets him into trouble once again. Presented by Sean Kelly

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys discover the ultimate coin-op man cave hidden on a farm in upstate New York, and Frank hatches a plan to help lonely collectors find love

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank visit the America's north east to fix up a '33 Ford Coupe

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake journeys on some of the most perilous routes in the world. In Alaska, Sue Perkins and Charley Boorman drive along the notorious Dalton Highway, a dirt track originally constructed in the 1970s as a supply road for the Trans-Alaska oil pipeline. The pair struggle to cope with the Arctic conditions and constant threat of avalanches, but receive help from experienced truckers who teach them some unwritten rules for surviving the road's many hazards

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The trio of motoring enthusiasts embark on another epic road trip, this time across western America in front-engined supercars. Jeremy Clarkson pilots the Lexus LFA, which has a spectacularly high-revving V10 powerplant, Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the muscular Dodge Viper, and James May opts for the latest incarnation of Aston Martin's Vanquish. On their journey from Las Vegas to Palm Springs via Los Angeles, the presenters tackle racing circuits and find themselves in a race against the police, before rushing to the Mexican border - and there's a terrifying penalty for the last man to arrive. Meanwhile, Fleetwood Mac drummer Mick Fleetwood rolls up his sleeves to thrash the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter continues his examination of how the car became an everyday part of people's lives, exploring the weird world of small vehicles. He discovers how austerity and fears of congestion led to European disasters in the shape of Britain's infamous three-wheelers, French death traps and German absurdities, and also takes to the battlefield to settle one of the greatest rivalries in car history

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef is invited to a family feast in Corfu and learns how to make authentic Greek food in restaurant kitchens around the island. Later, Rick heads for familiar surroundings in Majorca, where he worked as a teenage cook, and finds out what British tourists like to eat

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a race from London's Wembley Stadium to Milan's San Siro, the home of Italian football giants AC and Inter. A price restriction leads Jeremy to grab the keys to a Shelby Mustang GT500, which costs the equivalent of £35,000 in America and packs a supercharged punch of more than 650bhp. James and Richard pin their hopes on the pan-European rail network in a bid to secure the prize of tickets to a top-flight football match, with nothing but a long journey home awaiting the losers. On the test track, Jeremy puts Toyota's GT86 coupe through its paces, and its promise of entertaining rear-wheel-drive handling should put a smile on his face. Plus, singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald gets to grips with the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Kia Cee'd through a rigorous but peculiar road test featuring eels, curry, a rock legend, an American police officer and an unusual game of rugby involving James May. Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the Mastretta MXT, but worries about the repercussions of a comment he made in a previous episode about Mexico, the sports car's country of manufacture. Plus, three high-performance hatchbacks are driven with gusto around the test track, and Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton gets a second chance to smash the lap record

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks to a series of celebrity guests, as Tim Vine encounters a wet bra, Alice Levine barely attempts cricket and Russell Howard makes a convincing liger

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Stand-up star David O'Doherty, Pointless co-host Richard Osman, comedienne Susan Calman and weather presenter Carol Kirkwood join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the panel show, trying to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites boxer Nicola Adams, comedian Frankie Boyle and comedy actress Diane Morgan to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include magicians, losing one's keys and life itself

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites satirist Charlie Brooker, presenter Scarlett Moffatt and actress Pearl Mackie to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include crocodiles, mosquitoes and having one's hair cut

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Josh Widdicombe, Cariad Lloyd and Paul Sinha join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig considers various phenomena. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Alex Horne supervises the tasks as Mel Giedroyc's good nature is pushed to its limits, Joe Lycett shows no mercy to a camel, and Hugh Dennis gets a sleeping bag challenge

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Rhod Gilbert and Greg Davies negotiate a variety of unusual hazards as they travel through Nepal, a country with one of the world's highest rates of road traffic accidents. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Hugh Dennis undertake a perilous journey through the Peruvian Andes. programme.desc: The chef's final trip takes him to Morocco, where he samples the delights of couscous, tagines and mint tea in the country's bazaars, before travelling along the old silk route in south-eastern Turkey to taste kebabs, another of the Mediterranean's favourite foods

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. James focuses on passenger comfort, Richard tries to shorten response times by getting other drivers out of the way more effectively, an issue Jeremy tackles with the invention of what he calls the 'sports ambulance'. Their three very different creations are then put to the test with a series of severe and often messy challenges. Plus, Formula One driver Daniel Ricciardo is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests the new BMW M4 at the track, before switching to the German manufacturer's hybrid-powered i8 for a trip up the A1 to Whitby, North Yorkshire, where he decides which of the two he would rather take for the drive home. With Land Rover production ceasing in the UK later this year, Richard Hammond pays tribute to the 4x4 by attempting to drive one straight up the side of a dam, while James May puts the Mercedes AMG GT through its paces. Taking to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car are Will Smith and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The Taskmaster Greg Davies sends fear coursing through the veins of James Acaster, Jessica Knappett, Kerry Godliman, Phil Wang and Rhod Gilbert. In this edition, the challenges include an attempt to cheer up a grumpy traffic warden

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: A visit to Peles Castle in Romania, which was built in a medieval style in 1914 but is filled with modern technological innovations, including its own hydro-electric power plant

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Bohemia and Bavaria, beginning by exploring the art nouveau architecture of Czech Republic capital Prague. He then samples the sulphurous spa waters and wallows in peat and mud in Marianske Lazne, formerly known as Marienbad, before test driving a state-of-the-art passenger train made at the Skoda factory in Pilsen. Crossing the border into Germany, Michael encounters a fire-breathing dragon in Furth-im-Wald, and learns about an early 20th-century pioneer who laid the foundations for Munich's pre-eminence in science and technology

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Burton Constable Hall near Hull, which is home to a collection of treasures. Objects brought in to be valued include a ship's anchor that was found in a garden pond, a medal awarded to sailor for helping to recover Captain Scott's ship from the Antarctic in 1901, some 17th-century flat pack furniture, and a flimsy booklet that proved to be a profitable investment

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant returns for more train journeys through some of the world's most challenging terrain. He heads to the Arctic for a trip along the Alaska Railroad, but as he arrives in the port of Seward, it's not a great start as there are no trains running, so he ends up hitching a ride with a track inspector's car. He continues his journey on a freight train heading for Anchorage, Alaska's biggest city, and finally heads north on the Aurora passenger train to the town of Healy. Along the way he checks out the tiny village of Eklutna, which has great importance for the indigenous inhabitants, and goes for an intrepid fishing trip on a thawing frozen lake

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A small isolated country littered with strange concrete mushrooms and a secret Japanese facility that was once at the forefront of weapons technology

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An impressive compound commandeered and repurposed by a brutal regime and an eerie complex off the coast of Ireland that became the biggest of its kind

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: The life of Josef Mengele, the notorious demon doctor of Auschwitz, who conducted barbaric experiments on innocent victims - including children - in the concentration camp

programme.title: Mission: Kill Hitler
programme.desc: A plot to kill the fuhrer that remained secret until the late 1990s. Operation Foxley planned to murder Hitler during his daily morning walk at the Berghof, his Bavarian residence. Two soldiers parachuted into the area were supposed to find a perfect location to shoot Hitler and stop the devastating war. Based on the British Secret Services' declassified documents, using archives and fictionalised reconstitutions, this film recounts the full story and the detailed plan that should have led to the murder of Adolf Hitler, and explains why it failed.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys

With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War

programme.desc: Experts and witnesses analyse the events that unfolded when serious structural issues were discovered in a luxury apartment block in San Francisco, California

programme.title: World's Greatest Cars

programme.title: Fantomworks

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: An insight into how people care for bees, plus a look at how to build a trike that can carry a whole family and how to stop a bullet with a sheet of ceramic

programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers

programme.title: Massive Engineering Mistakes
programme.desc: Experts examine why America's first space station threatened to fall to earth, and how miscalculations meant strong winds could cause a New York skyscraper to collapse

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: American Pickers
